video:video.video:description: The head of the U.N.'s nuclear agency signaled Wednesday that Iranian nuclear enrichment sites would be visited by his inspectors, a key component in the interim deal between the United States and Iran to reach an end to the war.

video:video.video:title: Ex-national security adviser John Bolton pleads guilty to illegally retaining classified information
video:video.video:description: Bolton's plea agreement with the Justice Department may enable him to avoid time behind bars, but the judge ultimately will decide his punishment.

video:video.video:title: 'Office Romance' star Jennifer Lopez has not soured on workplace relationships
video:video.video:description: Jennifer Lopez says even if past relationships have not worked out, she hasn't closed the door on meeting future love interests at work. (June 5)

video:video.video:title: Watch: LA mayor candidate Spencer Pratt says he hopes incumbent Karen Bass is ‘ready’
video:video.video:description: “We can do debates every Friday if she would like,” Pratt told a group of reporters under the flash of cameras in his first remarks after results started rolling in.

video:video.video:title: US military posts video of a strike on an alleged drug boat, killing 2 in the eastern Pacific Ocean

video:video.video:description: Philippines President Ferdinand Marcos Jr and Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi were seen singing along to hits, including 'Hey Jude' by The Beatles and 'Imagine' by John Lennon during a state dinner in Tokyo on Thursday.

video:video.video:description: A group of more than 130 people in Barcelona gave a unique welcome to Pope Leo XIV on Tuesday night -- forming a human tower.

video:video.video:title: Former Federal Reserve Chairman Alan Greenspan dies at 100
video:video.video:description: Alan Greenspan, the jazz-playing U.S. Federal Reserve chair who was celebrated for engineering a decade of prosperity but later shared the blame for a devastating financial crisis, died Monday.

video:video.video:description: Leaders of the Group of Seven gathered on Tuesday with partner nations to discuss Russia's war in Ukraine and U.S.

video:video.video:description: Women are 40 times more likely to die in pregnancy or childbirth in Central African Republic than in the United States, according to the United Nations.

video:video.video:title: Chuck D remembers Peabo Bryson
video:video.video:description: At the premiere of Questlove's "Earth, Wind, and Fire" documentary, Chuck D reflects on the impact of singer Peabo Bryson. (June 3)

video:video.video:description: Rolling Stone co-editor-in-chief Shirley Halperin says Clive Davis was "responsible for so much of the soundtrack to our lives" and leaves a monumental legacy. (June 22)

video:video.video:description: Tough-on-crime outsider Aberaldo de la Espriella took the lead in Colombia's presidential race in the first round of voting Sunday night, setting up a runoff with Iván Cepeda, an ally of Colombia’s outgoing President Gustavo Petro who questioned the results of the election.

video:video.video:title: Paris Hilton backs Provo Canyon lawsuits, says change is needed

He remembers the pain and fear in his community in the eastern Congo city of Beni during the 2018-2020 Ebola outbreak, history's second-biggest with more than 3,400 reported cases and over 2,200 deaths.

video:video.video:description: In response to a question from The Associated Press during a meeting with heads of international news agencies, the Russian president also said his country is open for a compromise on Ukraine in line with understandings reached during his summit with President Trump in Alaska.

video:video.video:description: Nearly 75,000 voters in Ashton-in-Makerfield will cast ballots in a contest that may well pick the UK’s next prime minister, or plunge Britain's febrile politics into even more turmoil.

video:video.video:description: Damaging storms are sweeping through the Midwest, disrupting flights at Chicago airports and knocking out power to hundreds of thousands of customers.

video:video.video:title: Protesters, police clash in Bolivia after president signs law enabling a harsher crackdown
video:video.video:description: Protesters in Bolivia demanding the resignation of conservative President Rodrigo Paz hurled firecrackers, stones and sticks at police who responded with tear gas on Monday, leading to dozens of arrests as road blockades continue to paralyze the Andean nation.

video:video.video:title: Displaced Lebanese head to south Lebanon as US and Iran reach tentative deal
video:video.video:description: Thousands of displaced people and their belongings were seen on Monday heading south in Lebanon, shortly after a tentative deal was announced between the US and Iran.

video:video.video:description: U.S. and Iranian negotiators were in Switzerland on Sunday for talks on their interim agreement to end the Iran war.

video:video.video:title: Barack and Michelle Obama greeted the first visitors of the new presidential center on Juneteenth
video:video.video:description: Former President Barack Obama and former first lady Michelle Obama surprised the first 100 visitors to walk through the doors of Obama’s new presidential center, personally greeting them.

video:video.video:title: Huge fire burns at Moscow oil refinery after second Ukrainian attack in a week
video:video.video:description: Ukraine hit a major Moscow oil refinery for a second time in a week and disrupted commercial flights at Moscow airports in one of its biggest drone attacks since the start of the conflict more than four years ago, Russian officials said Thursday.

video:video.video:title: 'Death Stranding' film to diverge from game's story, director says
video:video.video:description: Michael Sarnoski says he's making good progress on "Death Stranding," the film based on the popular video game. (June 17)

video:video.video:description: Iran’s Foreign Minister says the tentative agreement to end the war with the United States would require Israel to withdraw from Lebanon — a condition Israel has already rejected.

video:video.video:title: Sánchez, Fujimori vote in runoff election as EU observers deploy across Peru
video:video.video:description: Peruvian presidential candidates Roberto Sánchez and Keiko Fujimori voted in Lima on Sunday as the nation went to the polls to decide their ninth president in just 10 years.

video:video.video:title: Helen Mirren leads 'Happy Birthday' song for King Charles at UK embassy in Rome
video:video.video:description: Dame Helen Mirren wished King Charles a 'Happy Birthday' while accepting inaugural UK-Italy GREAT Award in Rome. (June 18)

video:video.video:title: Asian shares shrug off Wall St blues following signing of US-Iran deal on ending the war
video:video.video:description: Shares have surged in Asia, with benchmarks in Japan and South Korea setting fresh records, after the U.S. and Iran signed their initial agreement aimed at ending the war.

video:video.video:title: Surprisingly smooth G7 summit appears to have given positive lift to ties between US and allies
video:video.video:description: Leaders at the Group of Seven summit on Wednesday threw their support behind U.S. President Donald Trump's tentative agreement with Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz and further extend a shaky ceasefire — even though he's offered scant specificity about how that would be implemented.

video:video.video:title: Taylor Swift at Knicks vs Spurs
video:video.video:description: Stars including Taylor Swift and A$AP Rocky attend Knicks' record-breaking rally over Spurs in Game 4 of the NBA Final.

video:video.video:title: Purported missiles were seen over the West Bank after a renewed exchange of fire between Israel and Iran.

video:video.video:description: Iranian Americans are torn over Iran’s participation in the World Cup, as some community members plan to watch Monday’s match and others are protesting outside the stadium where the team will play.

video:video.video:description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un observed sea trials of a new warship repaired after a failed launch last year and vowed to accelerate efforts to build a nuclear-armed navy, state media said Saturday, showcasing his expanding military capabilities ahead of a visit by Chinese leader Xi Jinping.

video:video.video:description: Ghana’s National Disaster Management Organisation (NADMO) carried out operations in Tesano, on the outskirts of the capital Accra, to save civilians from floodwaters Monday morning after heavy rains left much of the city inundated.

video:video.video:title: WHO chief reports 5 Ebola recoveries as a new treatment center opens in eastern Congo
video:video.video:description: The World Health Organization has reported five patients have recovered from a rare type of Ebola virus in eastern Congo.

video:video.video:description: Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin is defending his department's decision to deny a World Cup referee from Somalia entry to the U.S.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories June 5
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Friday, June 5th: UN force clears rubble in Lebanon town; Ukrainians react positively to Zelenskyy's letter to Putin; EU leaders meet to discuss the Balkans; so-called Leonardo's ferry helps river crossing in Italy.

video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV departed for Spain on Saturday for a 7-day Apostolic visit. Leo is expected to double down on his messages of unity amid polarization, peace as war rages, welcome for migrants and hope for young Spaniards in the era of artificial intelligence.

video:video.video:title: How Brexit broke British politics
video:video.video:description: Brexit fractured the European Union, and broke British politics. The U.K. is about to get its seventh prime minister since June 23, 2016, a decade ago Tuesday, when the country voted 52%-48% to leave the EU after more than four decades of membership.

video:video.video:title: New York says it is prepared for World Cup events
video:video.video:description: New York says it is prepared for the upcoming World Cup games and will deploy additional law enforcement to aid in the patrolling of major transit hubs and other special events.

video:video.video:title: Drone video shows rubble of collapsed buildings in Venezuela after earthquakes

video:video.video:description: Here's what makes the second season of "Paradise" special — and deeply personal — for Krys Marshall.

video:video.video:description: In an unprecedented move, the embattled chief prosecutor of the International Criminal Court was suspended from his duties late Monday, after the court’s oversight body referred British barrister Karim Khan for disciplinary proceedings.

video:video.video:description: Hong Kong authorities announced, just hours before the World Cup was set to kick off, the seizure of 230,000 suspected counterfeit items worth an estimated $20 million, including jerseys tied to the highly anticipated soccer tournament.

video:video.video:title: Air Canada pilot charged after allegedly flying without proper license between 2009 and 2025

video:video.video:title: A huge Viking textile production site is unearthed in Denmark
video:video.video:description: Archaeologists say they have unearthed a Viking Age textile production site in Danish Jutland. The sprawling 100,000-square-meter site features an area for processing flax, plus more than 80 pit houses - semi-submerged huts that were used as workshops and dwellings during the Viking Age

video:video.video:description: Vice President JD Vance said the Watergate scandal that brought down President Richard Nixon would have been a 12-hour blip in today's news cycle, and he drew parallels between Nixon and President Donald Trump — arguing both were targeted by “deep state” forces.

video:video.video:title: Voters head to polls in Iowa
video:video.video:description: Iowa voters head to the polls to settle one of the Democrat party’s last competitive U.S. Senate primaries (AP Video by Hannah Fingerhut)

video:video.video:title: Tremor felt in General Santos, Philippines, two days after deadly quake hit the country
video:video.video:description: Rescuers who were searching ruined buildings in the southern Philippines after one of the strongest earthquakes to hit the country, felt another smaller earthquake as they were working on Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Trump believes Iran's Supreme Leader has signed off on emerging deal
video:video.video:description: "I understand the answer is yes" President Donald Trump said when asked by reporters if Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei has agreed to the deal aimed at winding down the conflict.

video:video.video:title: Shaggy on keeping his music unapologetically ‘Shaggy’
video:video.video:description: Speaking about his latest album, ‘Lottery,’ Shaggy says he creates music that stays true to himself rather than chasing trends, and believes that authenticity is what continues to resonate with audiences.

video:video.video:title: Sharp drops in Big Tech companies pull the Nasdaq lower
video:video.video:description: A sell-off in big technology companies spread from Asia back to the U.S., pulling the Nasdaq composite down 3% in early trading.

video:video.video:title: Panini sticker swapping an 'iconic part of any World Cup'
video:video.video:description: The stickers depicting players and teams in the World Cup have been around since 1970. More than 50 years later, fans young and old swap stickers to help each other fill their keepsake albums.

video:video.video:title: Labour Party’s Andy Burnham wins UK special election, setting up likely push to oust PM Keir Starmer
video:video.video:description: Andy Burnham is a political insider turned outsider who aims to be Britain’s next prime minister. Burnham is expected to challenge Prime Minister Keir Starmer after winning a seat in Parliament in a special election he hailed as a “turning point” for U.K. politics.

video:video.video:title: Hot mic captures Giorgia Meloni telling G7 leaders that she quit smoking one month ago
video:video.video:description: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni told fellow G7 leaders on Tuesday she had quit smoking. While waiting for US President Donald Trump, French President Emmanuel Macron and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy to arrive for a meeting, G7 leaders struck up a conversation about smoking.

video:video.video:title: World Cup fans go crazy for ‘Canadian clapper’ maple leaf-shaped noisemakers given out in Toronto
video:video.video:description: A little red noisemaker shaped like a maple leaf has become all the rage in Toronto during the World Cup. (AP video: Mike Householder)

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories June 21

video:video.video:title: Quarantine comes to an end for the last of the hantavirus ship passengers in Nebraska
video:video.video:description: The last eight American passengers who endured 42 days in a specialized hospital quarantine unit after exposure to an unusual hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship that killed three people have left the Nebraska facility.

video:video.video:title: Delirious celebrations in New York as Knicks win first NBA championship in 53 years
video:video.video:description: The Knicks rallied from a 16-point deficit to beat the Spurs in San Antonio in Game 5 of the NBA Finals, turning pensive crowds euphoric at watch parties outside the team's Madison Square Garden arena and in parks and streets across the city.

video:video.video:description: Actor Ewan McGregor says his role in the beloved film “Trainspotting” helped launch his career and set a tone for filmmaking.

video:video.video:description: Russian President Vladimir Putin on Friday rejected a proposal by Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy for a face-to-face negotiation. Speaking at the St.

video:video.video:title: Lufthansa employees injured in Boeing nose gear incident at Frankfurt airport
video:video.video:description: A number of employees of German airline Lufthansa were injured after the nose gear of a stationary aircraft unexpectedly retracted at Frankfurt airport on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Israeli strikes leave widespread damage in Nabatiyeh as ceasefire appears to be holding in Lebanon
video:video.video:description: Israeli strikes left widespread damage in Nabatiyeh in southern Lebanon as a ceasefire appeared to be holding on Sunday. (Production by Ali Sharafeddine)

video:video.video:title: 50 Cent credits director for success of 'Sean Combs: The Reckoning'
video:video.video:description: Executive producer 50 Cent wants to make it clear that director Alexandria Stapleton is behind the success of "Sean Combs: The Reckoning," the Netflix documentary series about Diddy's legal battles.

video:video.video:description: Pope Leo is set to visit the Canary Islands this weekend to show defending migrants remains a priority issue.

video:video.video:description: Rescuers searched ruined buildings in the southern Philippines on Wednesday to ensure no one was still trapped two days after one of the strongest earthquakes to hit the country in a half-century killed at least 37 people and displaced more than 32,000.

video:video.video:title: Burmese authorities torch $600 million in seized heroin, meth and other drugs
video:video.video:description: Thick clouds of black smoke billowed into the sky on the outskirts of Myanmar's largest city Friday as authorities burned more than 50 tons of heroin, opium, ketamine, methamphetamine, marijuana and crystal meth — some $600 million of confiscated illegal drugs destroyed nationwide.

video:video.video:title: Matty Matheson on saying goodbye to 'The Bear'
video:video.video:description: While attending the "Toy Story 5" premiere, Matty Matheson says he's thankful for the lifelong friends he's made on the set of "The Bear." (June 9)

video:video.video:description: Tehran residents expressed mixed views on Thursday over more U.S. airstrikes and the prospects of an agreement. (AP video shot by Mohsen Ganji)

video:video.video:description: Jennifer Lopez and Brett Goldstein joke about whether they were tempted to have a "showmance" when promoting their raunchy new comedy "Office Romance" in London Wednesday.

video:video.video:description: Milly Alcock stars as Kara Zor-El, better known as "Supergirl", in the latest installment of James Gunn's expanding DC Universe. (June 22)

video:video.video:title: South Africa marks 50 years since Soweto uprising by students, but challenges linger for its youth
video:video.video:description: The events of June 16, 1976 are now commemorated annually as Youth Day. Fifty years after the uprising, many from South Africa’s “born free” generation, those born after the end of apartheid in 1994, still face inequality, unemployment and poverty.

video:video.video:description: Dennis Dahmer Sr., whose voting rights activist father was killed by the Ku Klux Klan, wants to remind people the price his family and other families have paid for Black people to have the right to vote.

video:video.video:description: Speaking on the sidelines of the G7 summit in France, President Donald Trump said the Strait of Hormuz will be fully reopened and toll-free by Friday as the U.S. and Iran prepare to sign a ceasefire agreement and begin talks on Tehran’s nuclear program.

video:video.video:description: The lead negotiator of the Iranian delegation, Mohammad Bagher Qalibaf, insisted on Monday the Strait of Hormuz will be managed by Iran and would follow international laws.

video:video.video:description: Millions of soccer fans from across the globe are expected to descend on 16 cities in the U.S., Mexico and Canada during the World Cup games this summer with some already planning how to stay hydrated.

video:video.video:description: Eight protesters accused by the Justice Department of having ties to antifa have been sentenced to decades in federal prison over a shooting outside a Texas immigration detention center.

video:video.video:description: Timelapse of workers covering signage at the Kennedy Center before removing Trump's name, after a judge ruled it was illegally added to the building.

video:video.video:description: Beyond a few facilities that treat mostly farm animals, just two veterinary clinics for common house pets remain operational across Gaza.

video:video.video:description: Voters in California are weighing in on who should lead the nation’s most populous state, where there is no clear leader among candidates vying to advance in the race to succeed Democratic Gov.

video:video.video:description: A rural area of Northern California experienced its strongest earthquake since 1940 on Wednesday morning, but it caused only mild shaking with no immediate reports of damage or injuries.

video:video.video:description: A small plane crashed in the middle of a highway in southern Texas. Crews rushed Tuesday night to pull out people from the aircraft as flames burst from the plane.

video:video.video:description: The future of the famous Rift Valley flamingos is becoming uncertain as the ecology of lakes they rely on are changing.

video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV called Monday for newfound respect for the rights of migrants and international law during an address to the Spanish parliament that signaled a new level of acceptance of the Catholic Church in the public sphere in the overwhelmingly secular country.

video:video.video:description: Dwayne Johnson performs "You're Welcome," as he joins "Moana" stars old and new, surprising fans at a London event celebrating the upcoming live action remake.

video:video.video:title: A look at the hidden treasures of Barcelona's Sagrada Familia, ahead of Pope Leo's visit
video:video.video:description: The unique grandeur of the Sagrada Familia — the stone somehow carved to look lush as flora, the sandcastle-like spires, the color-flooded interior — means that even those who work and worship at Barcelona’s world famous basilica each and every day can still be surprised with some fresh wonder.

video:video.video:title: 'Nobody cares': Trump interrupts Chris Wright as he attempts to get dates right
video:video.video:description: During a signing ceremony, the Energy Secretary offered an anecdote attempting to tie Einstein’s work to that of Trump’s uncle, who was an MIT professor.

video:video.video:title: Historic significance of Juneteenth celebrated by Galveston, Texas residents
video:video.video:description: Residents in Galveston, Texas turned out to celebrate the deep roots of Juneteenth in that city. June 19, 1865 marks the day when the last enslaved African Americans in the Confederacy learned of their freedom there.

video:video.video:title: Fans celebrate after Paraguay stuns Germany for the biggest upset of the 2026 World Cup
video:video.video:description: Paraguay fans celebrated in Foxborough, Massachusetts on Monday, singing, dancing and marching with drums, after Paraguay stunned Germany in a penalty shootout for the biggest upset of the 2026 World Cup.

video:video.video:title: Some facts about the strongest earthquakes ever recorded
video:video.video:description: Powerful back-to-back earthquakes struck Venezuela on Wednesday evening, causing widespread damage, collapsing buildings and sending panicked residents into the streets.

video:video.video:title: Rex Heuermann sentenced to life in prison without parole in New York’s Gilgo Beach serial killings
video:video.video:description: A Long Island architect who lived a secret life as the Gilgo Beach serial killer was sentenced to life in prison without parole Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Soccer fans spend time with Tim Howard on World Cup field
video:video.video:description: A few lucky fans stood pitchside with former American soccer player Tim Howard as the U.S. began its World Cup run with a 4-1 win over Paraguay last week.

video:video.video:title: International rescue teams and aid arrive in Venezuela to help earthquake response
video:video.video:description: Rescue teams have begun arriving in the hard-hit Venezuelan city of La Guaira to assist with search and rescue operations after a pair of powerful earthquakes.

video:video.video:title: Trump arrives at the Octagon to celebrate 80th birthday with UFC show on White House lawn
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump celebrated his 80th birthday Sunday with cage fighting on the White House lawn.

video:video.video:title: Xi makes rare visit to North korea as he and Kim seek stronger China-North Korea ties
video:video.video:description: China's President Xi Jinping and North Korea's leader Kim Jong Un underscored their commitment to deepen cooperation and rebuild their complicated traditional alliance.

video:video.video:title: Royal family attends Trooping of the Colour parade in London to mark King's official birthday
video:video.video:description: The parade was attended by Catherine, Princess of Wales along with her children, Prince George, Princess Charlotte and Prince Louis.

video:video.video:title: Police officer rescues woman whose vehicle fell into sinkhole in Virginia
video:video.video:description: A woman whose vehicle fell into a water-filled sinkhole has a Norfolk police officer and several others to thank for coming to her rescue.

video:video.video:title: US military disable oil tanker violating blockade in the Gulf of Oman, CENTCOM says
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military said Wednesday that it disabled an oil tanker in the Gulf of Oman. The Palau-flagged vessel was suspected of trying to violate the American blockade, the latest action by the Trump administration to try to push Tehran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Ike Barinholtz teases Madonna cameo in season two of ‘The Studio’
video:video.video:description: Speaking at the Peabody Awards, Ike Barinholtz discussed Madonna’s cameo in the upcoming second season of “The Studio," adding, "They wanted to go bigger and better in season two."

video:video.video:title: Trump's new attitude toward allies buoyed by their praise for Iran deal
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump isn't fond of international meetings of world leaders. But he changed his tune at the G7 summit after earning support from his counterparts for a tentative agreement he struck with Iran to end the war.

video:video.video:title: Ecuadorian artisans struggle to preserve traditional hand-woven strainers
video:video.video:description: Artisans in Guangopolo, Ecuador, are struggling to keep the traditional craft of weaving horsehair sieves alive.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories June 11
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Thursday, June 11th: Trump backs off new strikes against Iran; Labor Department reports big jump in producer prices; Trump announces new pick for director of national intelligence; World Cup fever strikes Mexico as tournament begins.

video:video.video:title: Larger-than-life looks at fashion graduate show
video:video.video:description: When it comes to dreaming big - and demonstrating it - few things are on the scale of the graduate fashion show for London arts college, Central Saint Martins. (June 5)

video:video.video:title: Lionel Messi's family pleads for 'humanity' as the Argentina captain's father undergoes treatment
video:video.video:description: Lionel Messi's family pleads for 'humanity' as the Argentina captain's father undergoes treatment

video:video.video:title: Morocco fans gather in Monterrey before knockout clash with the Netherlands
video:video.video:description: Morocco supporters gathered at Monterrey's Macroplaza on Sunday, creating a festive atmosphere ahead of their team's Round of 32 World Cup match against the Netherlands.

video:video.video:title: Cody Johnson pays tribute to 'hero' Alan Jackson
video:video.video:description: "You can't say country music is without saying Alan Jackson," says Cody Johnson on the eve of the country superstar's farewell concert to be held Saturday in Nashville.

video:video.video:title: Sweden to ban mobiles in schools as part of back-to-books strategy
video:video.video:description: Long championed as a leader in tech and digital adoption, Sweden is set to ban mobile phones in schools from the next academic year as part of a broad reversal on the use of screens in classrooms.
video:video.video:tag: Organization for Economic Co-operation and Development

video:video.video:title: Israeli military strikes Beirut suburbs in the lead-up to anticipated US-Iran deal
video:video.video:description: The Israeli military said it launched strikes on Beirut on Sunday targeting Hezbollah infrastructure, despite ongoing efforts to negotiate an end to the U.S.-Iran war.

video:video.video:title: Soccer legend Beckham gets star on Hollywood Walk of Fame
video:video.video:description: Soccer legend David Beckham received a star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame on Friday. As the World Cup dominates sports headlines, Beckham is largely credited with helping broaden the popularity and reach of the sport in the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Rising gas prices could mean pricier summer for US boaters
video:video.video:description: U.S. boaters, like drivers, are feeling the pinch from the Iran war with higher gas costs. (AP video: Mike Householder and Dee-Ann Durbin)

video:video.video:title: Cody Johnson: 'The best is yet to come'
video:video.video:description: Country music star Cody Johnson looks forward to release of new album "Banks of the Trinity," says it signals where he's headed as an artist.

video:video.video:title: Sirens sound over Kuwait as Iran targets country with drones and missiles
video:video.video:description: Sirens sounded in Kuwait City early on Sunday after both Bahrain and Kuwait said Iran targeted their nations with drone and missile fire.

video:video.video:title: Signs of revival of interest in faith among young people in Madrid, as pope visits
video:video.video:description: Traveling to Spain in June and France in September, Pope Leo XIV will find traditionally Catholic but now staunchly secular countries with an abundance of historic churches but few people in the pews.

video:video.video:title: Scenes of anguish as relatives bury Lebanese family killed in Israeli strike
video:video.video:description: Six members of the al-Abdallah family - father Hassan, mother Hanan, and four of their children, aged between 20 and six – died in Monday's air strike that demolished their home in Khiam, along the border with Israel.

video:video.video:title: Only on AP: Israeli ambassador to France says 'choice was Iran's to resume hostilities'
video:video.video:description: Israel's ambassador to France on Monday said the "choice was Iran's to resume hostilities" between Israel and Iran as both sides traded fire for the first time since the U.S. agreed to a ceasefire with Tehran two months ago.

video:video.video:title: Cat steals the show during Romeo and Juliet ballet in Turkey
video:video.video:description: A cat stole the spotlight during a performance by the Imperial Russian Ballet Company, casually sauntering onto the stage as dancers performed a scene from Romeo and Juliet in western Turkey last week.

video:video.video:title: Mexico, U.S. reopen sterile fly plant to fight screwworm as outbreak disrupts cattle trade
video:video.video:description: Mexico and the United States reopened Saturday a sterile fly production facility in the southern Mexican state of Chiapas, expanding efforts to contain the New World screwworm, a livestock pest that has disrupted cross-border cattle trade.

video:video.video:title: 'Dancing Devils' chase children to their first communion in Venezuela
video:video.video:description: Children and spectators on Thursday were chased by 'dancing devils,' parishioners of the Carayaca church in Tarmas in Venezuela, as they took part in Corpus Christi celebrations (AP Video/Andry Rincon)

video:video.video:title: Lebanon and Israel extend fragile ceasefire, create security zones that exclude Hezbollah
video:video.video:description: In a joint statement released after a fourth round of U.S.-mediated talks at the State Department, the two sides said the ceasefire “is contingent on a complete cessation of Hezbollah fire and the evacuation of all Hezbollah operatives” from areas south of the Litani River, which is roughly 30 kilom

video:video.video:title: Animals at the Brookfield Zoo in Chicago gear up ahead of the World Cup
video:video.video:description: From its red river hogs to its Addax, Ike, to the American bison and its Grevy’s zebra, the animals at the zoo enjoyed soccer-themed enrichment activities last week ahead of the games.

video:video.video:title: Mexico fans gather at foot of Angel of Independence after World Cup win
video:video.video:description: Mexico City's iconic Ángel de la Independencia became the center of celebrations Thursday night after Mexico defeated South Africa 2-0 in the opening match of the 2026 World Cup.

video:video.video:title: Hong Kong starts to promote its first five-year plan in line with mainland China
video:video.video:description: Hong Kong has launched a public consultation for its first five-year plan in a politically symbolic step that brings it closer to mainland China’s development approach.

video:video.video:title: David Hockney dies at 88
video:video.video:description: Acclaimed British artist, David Hockney, whose paintings of pools shimmering in the Los Angeles sunshine became icons of 20th-century art, has died at 88. (June 12)

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo is greeted by enthusiastic crowds at soccer stadium in Madrid
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV visited the home of Real Madrid Football Club, the Santiago Bernabéu Stadium, where around 80,000 people burst into wild cheers as he entered the arena in an electric golf cart.

video:video.video:title: Milan Fashion Week - menswear trends
video:video.video:description: In complicated times, Milan designers went lighter — if not in materials, then in silhouette. Amid economic uncertainty, geopolitical tensions and a sweltering Milan Fashion Week, designers largely stripped things back for next summer, embracing clean lines and pared-down looks.

video:video.video:title: People experiencing homelessness seek respite from the heat at a cooling center in Georgia
video:video.video:description: People without access to housing are getting a break from the heat at a homeless shelter in Georgia.

video:video.video:title: Demonstrators protest FIFA policy prohibiting pre-Islamic Revolution Iranian flags into World Cup matches
video:video.video:description: Waving the flags of pre-Islamic Revolution Iran, hundreds of protesters opposed to the current government demonstrated outside SOFI Stadium in Inglewood, California where the team's World Cup matches will be initially played.

video:video.video:title: South African inmates embrace art in prison to curb repeat offending
video:video.video:description: When most people think of prison, they picture steel bars, locked gates and lost freedom. But at a correctional facility in Johannesburg, South Africa’s largest city, visitors are greeted by something unexpected: an art gallery

video:video.video:title: King Charles III will not live at Buckingham Palace after completion of costly refurbishment
video:video.video:description: King Charles III will not live at Buckingham Palace after the completion of a 10-year, 369 million-pound ($487 million) refurbishment program as the monarchy seeks to increase public access to the historic building that has been the center of royal life for almost 200 years.

video:video.video:title: Russian attack sets fire to centuries-old religious site in Kyiv and kills rescuers in Kharkiv
video:video.video:description: A large-scale Russian attack on Ukraine killed rescue personnel in Kharkiv and four people in the capital Kyiv on Monday as strikes set apartment buildings ablaze and sparked a fire at the Kyiv-Pechersk Lavra, one of the country’s most significant religious landmarks.

video:video.video:title: Polls open in Ethiopia's election which is widely expected to be won by the ruling party
video:video.video:description: Polls opened Monday in Ethiopia in an election that is widely expected to be won by the ruling party.

video:video.video:title: Vance says talks in Switzerland with Iranian officials set 'good foundation' for deal to end war
video:video.video:description: Vice President JD Vance said Monday peace talks with Iran created a “good foundation for a successful final deal” to end the war that began at the end of February.

video:video.video:title: European heatwave triggers health warnings from London to Milan as temperatures rise even higher
video:video.video:description: Large parts of western Europe were baking Wednesday as a "heat dome" brought extreme conditions that many forecasters warned could present a risk to life.

video:video.video:title: Soccer fans spend time with Tim Howard on World Cup field
video:video.video:description: A few lucky fans stood pitchside with former American soccer player Tim Howard as the U.S. began its World Cup run with a 4-1 win over Paraguay last week.

video:video.video:title: Guinness crowns Canberra town crier as the world's loudest person
video:video.video:description: Guinness World Records last week acknowledged 58-year-old Joseph McGrail-Bateup from Canberra, Australia resident as the world’s loudest person.

video:video.video:title: Louisiana residents fill sandbags to prepare for Tropical Storm Arthur
video:video.video:description: Louisiana residents are filling bags to prepare for Tropical Storm Arthur, the first tropical cyclone of the season which is expected to produce 20 inches (50 centimeters) of rainfall in some areas.

video:video.video:title: Archaeologists uncover pieces of history from the Battle of Bunker Hill
video:video.video:description: On the same hill where bakers, butchers and candlemakers battled the most powerful army in the world, veterans of a new generation went on their knees in the dirt – searching for artifacts from the conflict 251 years ago.

video:video.video:title: Socceroos fans feeling positive after Australia wins 2-0 against Turkey in the World Cup
video:video.video:description: Many celebrated on Granville Street in downtown Vancouver where lines stretched from one bar to the next.

video:video.video:title: Octavia Spencer (and the USA) are embracing World Cup fans
video:video.video:description: Octavia Spencer doesn't know the rules of soccer, but she's quickly learning as World Cup fever grips the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Trump says US 'must' respond after Iran shot down US Army helicopter near Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump blamed Iran for shooting down a U.S. Army helicopter near the Strait of Hormuz on Tuesday and said the United States must respond to the attack.

video:video.video:title: Israelis and Iranians weigh what comes next as countries appear to pause strikes
video:video.video:description: Israelis and Iranians showed conflicted emotions on Tuesday as the two countries appeared to end the latest exchange of strikes; some expressed optimism, while others remained skeptical about what comes next.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV visits a welfare center in Madrid run by the Caritas charity
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV visited a welfare center run by the Caritas charity in a deprived area of Madrid on Saturday.

video:video.video:title: Damage forces postponement of opening of 'cave' installation on Paris' oldest bridge
video:video.video:description: Parts of the installation, which looks like an inflatable cave, appeared ripped and were seen fluttering in high winds, with poor weather the likely culprit behind the damage, the project's media spokesperson said.

video:video.video:title: Only on AP: Under Notre Dame cathedral, a 'dig of the century' unearths 1,700 years of history
video:video.video:description: Archaeologists are digging beneath Notre Dame Cathedral to explore as far back as Roman Paris from 2,000 years ago.

video:video.video:title: Hong Kong artist braves police attention with commemoration on Tiananmen crackdown anniversary eve
video:video.video:description: A performance artist on Wednesday sought to display a thin red thread in downtown Hong Kong to remember the victims of Beijing’s 1989 Tiananmen Square crackdown, 37 years on from the event.

video:video.video:title: Trump says US military strike killed leader of Tren de Aragua gang with help from Venezuela
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Friday that a “swift and lethal kinetic” U.S. strike has killed Héctor Rusthenford Guerrero Flores, whom he called “the infamous leader” of the Tren de Aragua gang.

video:video.video:title: Three Siberian tiger cubs born at Ljubljana Zoo
video:video.video:description: Zoo staff members are monitoring the cubs’ development through cameras installed inside the enclosure and say that 4-year-old Arisa has adapted well to motherhood.

video:video.video:title: Europe's record heat has overwhelmed Paris mortuaries and left families in distress
video:video.video:description: Public Health France said there were more than 1,200 deaths last Wednesday, when France registered its hottest-ever day.

video:video.video:title: Bill Gates arrives to testify before panel investigating the Jeffrey Epstein files
video:video.video:description: Bill Gates has arrived on Capitol Hill to appear before a congressional panel investigating the Jeffrey Epstein files, becoming the latest powerful figure linked to the disgraced financier to testify.

video:video.video:title: Hindu artisans in Karachi prepare Tazias as part of religious festival of Ashura
video:video.video:description: In a bustling core colonial area of Pakistan’s port city of Karachi, a minority Hindu family is busy preparing Tazia, a symbolic tomb of Imam Hussain, a grandson of Prophet Muhammad to commemorate the historic martyrdom alongside the country's Muslim majority.

video:video.video:title: Trump is scrapping $1.8B fund meant to compensate president's allies, Blanche says
video:video.video:description: The Trump administration is scrapping plans to create a $1.8 billion fund meant to compensate allies of the president, the Justice Department's top official said Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Colombia's vote may reshape the Amazon's future as political winds shift across Latin America
video:video.video:description: Abelardo de la Espriella, set to be Colombia’s president, has intensified debate over the future of the Amazon.

video:video.video:title: Starmer hosts Zelenskyy, Macron and Merz for talks in London
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy insisted Sunday that Europe must play a key role in any negotiations to end the war with Russia as he arrived for talks in London with staunch European allies in the wake of a Russian drone strike that killed three people.

video:video.video:title: Cape Verde fans in joyous celebrations outside stadium after making World Cup knockout round
video:video.video:description: Cape Verde fans celebrated outside Houston Stadium on Friday evening, after making the World Cup knockout round.

video:video.video:title: Hugh Jackman enters his pirate era
video:video.video:description: Hugh Jackman has signed on to play pirate Long John Silver in a new adaptation of "Treasure Island," directed by Ridley Scott.

video:video.video:title: Ukraine drones hit St. Petersburg oil terminal ahead of Putin's visit to city
video:video.video:description: Ukraine says its long-range drones struck an oil terminal in St. Petersburg, Russia and set it ablaze as the city hosted an annual international economic forum promoted by President Vladimir Putin.

video:video.video:description: Waving Argentine flags and wearing the team’s famous striped jersey, Bangladeshi fans cheered as the team of Lionel Messi beat Jordan 3-1 to complete a sweep of their group.

video:video.video:title: Climate change is fueling more extreme weather
video:video.video:description: From stronger hurricanes to longer droughts and heavier rainfall, a warming planet is intensifying weather extremes and making them more frequent. (AP Video: Aya Diab)

video:video.video:title: Donkey therapy helps improve patient wellbeing at French hospital
video:video.video:description: Donkeys are helping psychiatric patients at a hospital in France. A unique therapy programme uses the animals to help people with mental health conditions recover.

video:video.video:title: Boots Riley on the bigger message behind 'I Love Boosters'
video:video.video:description: Boots Riley wants "I Love Boosters" to spark discussions around "how the working class can have power."

video:video.video:title: Football players in Gaza return to training, trying to make up for lost time during the war
video:video.video:description: On a modest soccer field in Gaza City surrounded by hollowed out buildings and rubble, Palestinian soccer players gathered for their training session, as the war had destroyed most of the fields where they used to play.

video:video.video:description: Police were pelted with chairs, cans, rocks and flares late Tuesday by some of the hundreds who attended a protest in the southern English coast city of Southampton, where Henry Nowak was killed in December.

video:video.video:title: Pottery wheel becomes lifeline for blind war veteran in Ukraine
video:video.video:description: A Ukrainian war veteran who was blinded in combat is now teaching others pottery, a craft he learned during rehabilitation.

video:video.video:title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang calls on Korean gaming superstar Faker during Seoul visit
video:video.video:description: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang began his five-day to South Korea on Friday by calling on international gaming superstar Faker.

video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Monday, June 29th: Secretary of State Rubio meets with German counterpart in Washington; Flooding makes some roads in Accra impassable; Palestinian officials visit archeological site they say Israel is trying to wrest control of; Music stars among the mourners at the funeral of

video:video.video:title: World Cup fans seek photos and some luck at Philadelphia's Rocky statue
video:video.video:description: Thousands of World Cup fans visit Philadelphia's Rocky statue for photos each day. Some locals believe the statue brings bad luck to any team whose jersey is placed on it.

video:video.video:title: Tropical Storm Mekkhala continues to drench Japan with heavy downpours
video:video.video:description: The heavy rain caused disruptions to the country's railway network, prompting severe delays and service suspensions in various regions.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories June 8
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Monday, June 8th: Iran and Israel trade weapons fire; Houthi rebels claim missile fired at Israel from Yemen; Powerful earthquake in Philippines; Toledo, Ohio police search for mass shooting suspects.

video:video.video:title: Leon Thomas: 'R&B is home for me'
video:video.video:description: Leon Thomas explains the impact R&B music has had on his life and artistry. He was honored with the ASCAP Vanguard Award.

video:video.video:title: Robots used by Amazon keep packages moving quickly
video:video.video:description: Look inside Amazon's robotics lab in Massachusetts, where a fleet of robots keeps packages moving swiftly. (AP video: Sydney Roth)

video:video.video:title: Firefighters battle wildfire that destroyed homes in Spokane, Washington
video:video.video:description: High winds drove a wildfire into a Spokane neighborhood, forcing the evacuation of about 1,200 people and potentially damaging or destroying up to 15 structures, according to fire officials.

video:video.video:title: Strong winds damage buildings and tear trees in Wisconsin as US Midwest braces for severe storms
video:video.video:description: Aerial footage shows cleanup crew cleaning up debris, roofs torn off buildings and trees split in half after a strong storm swept through a Wisconsin city.

video:video.video:title: Orphaned children in Gaza put a huge strain on those left to care for them
video:video.video:description: As the death toll in Gaza surpassed 73,000 on Sunday, the overwhelming number of orphaned children who have lost one or both parents have put a huge strain on those left to care for them.

video:video.video:title: What a journey - meet Feliks the eagle, back in Serbia after Middle East ordeal
video:video.video:description: He's safe, nestled in the branches now. But Feliks has been on an adventure. The young eastern imperial eagle set off on his first migratory flight last year, only to be caught by poachers in the Middle East and illegally sold.

video:video.video:title: Lebanese Americans do what they can to assist friends, family suffering due to attacks on homeland
video:video.video:description: Lebanese Americans pray, protest and raise money to help friends and family impacted by the fighting between Israel and Hezbollah. (AP video: Mike Householder)

video:video.video:title: Protests continue in Albania over tourism project linked to Trump’s son-in-law
video:video.video:description: Protests continued in Albania, Monday, against a tourism project linked to U.S. President Donald Trump’s son-in-law, Jared Kushner. (AP Video shot by Eri Xhabafti)

video:video.video:title: Police: Suspect who took 10 people hostage in California standoff has been shot and killed
video:video.video:description: A man held 10 people hostage inside a California office building before the FBI shot and killed him early Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Dallas Stadium unveils World Cup transformation ahead of opening matches
video:video.video:description: Dallas Stadium will host the first North Texas World Cup match on June 14th between the Netherlands and Japan.

video:video.video:title: Oz says he trusts Trump’s judgement when asked why Pulte is qualified for DNI
video:video.video:description: Mehmet Oz, head of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services, had his turn at the White House briefing room podium but had little to offer when reporters asked about Bill Pulte's nomination as acting director of national intelligence.

video:video.video:title: Deadly Russian attack hits Kyiv and damages centuries-old religious site
video:video.video:description: A large-scale Russian attack on Ukraine on Monday killed rescue workers and sparked a fire Monday at one of the country’s most significant religious landmarks, a monastic complex of golden domes in the heart of Kyiv.

video:video.video:title: Brazil fans pack Copacabana Beach for World Cup opener against Morocco
video:video.video:description: Facing pressure to win its first World Cup title since 2002, five-time champion Brazil was outplayed early and needed Vinícius Júnior's 32nd-minute goal to gain a 1-1 draw against Morocco on Saturday in a pulsating, high-profile group match.

video:video.video:title: Fans of Spain and soccer fill the streets of Atlanta before World Cup match
video:video.video:description: Thousands of fans have taken to the streets of Atlanta, throwing a raucous rolling party in support of Spain's soccer team.

video:video.video:title: Lebanese in Tyre and Israelis on border reflect on ceasefire
video:video.video:description: Residents in Lebanon and Israelis along the border on Saturday voiced uncertainty over the interim peace agreement between the Iran-backed Hezbollah militant group and Israel.

video:video.video:title: Author Ann Patchett drives the literary conversation
video:video.video:description: From her bookstore in Nashville, Tennessee, author Ann Patchett is showcasing other literary greats, while continuing her work as a prize-winning novelist with several bestsellers.

video:video.video:title: Conservation group donates equipment to help prevent human-gorilla transmission of Ebola
video:video.video:description: As Uganda battles a deadly outbreak of the Ebola virus, conservationists are increasing efforts to protect the country's mountain gorillas.
video:video.video:tag: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

video:video.video:title: Tri-state area expected to endure dangerous heat wave through end of holiday week
video:video.video:description: People were seen cooling off at Coney Island beach on Monday as officials have already placed New York under extreme heat watch.

video:video.video:title: Armenian PM and opposition vote in election, amid Russian pressure to prevent drift toward West
video:video.video:description: Armenians are voting Sunday in parliamentary elections as the incumbent government, under mounting Russian pressure, seeks to loosen ties with Moscow and deepen cooperation with the West.

video:video.video:title: Díaz-Canel announces economic reforms to attract investment, involve Cubans abroad
video:video.video:description: Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel announced Friday a package of economic reforms aimed at attracting investment, expanding participation by Cubans living abroad in the economy and decentralizing parts of the country’s administration.

video:video.video:title: Albanians keep protesting against Kushner-linked resort project
video:video.video:description: Thousands of Albanians continued to protest against a coastal development project linked to Donald Trump’s son-in-law Jared Kushner, on Saturday.

video:video.video:title: Meloni slams Trump's claim she 'begged' for a photo with him
video:video.video:description: Italy’s government on Friday slammed U.S. President Donald Trump’s claim that Premier Giorgia Meloni had “begged” for a one-on-one meeting with him during the recent G7 summit, a pushback that suggested the longtime U.S. ally had had enough of Trump’s boasting.

video:video.video:title: Police find six dead in Mechanicville, New York apartment
video:video.video:description: Police say four children and two adults were found dead inside an apartment in Mechanicville, New York, while police were conducting a welfare check on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: The Chinese empress dress trend soaring in Beijing
video:video.video:description: Studios near the Forbidden City in Beijing are transforming visitors into empresses from ancient China as young people explore a revival of traditional culture.

video:video.video:title: Teen dies after being thrown from horse-drawn carriage in New York’s Central Park
video:video.video:description: A teenager died after being thrown to the ground when a Central Park carriage horse bolted away from its driver.

video:video.video:title: Netanyahu vows Israeli forces will maintain occupied 'security zone' in southern Lebanon
video:video.video:description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said on Thursday that Israel's military will stay in southern Lebanon, where it has occupied up to 10 kilometers (six miles) from the border.

video:video.video:title: Luigi Mangione will assert psychiatric defense in murder case in UnitedHealthcare CEO's killing
video:video.video:description: Luigi Mangione will assert a psychiatric defense at his state murder trial in the killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson.

video:video.video:title: Mayhem mars euphoria as New York City celebrates the Knicks' first championship in 53 years
video:video.video:description: Outside Madison Square Garden, a crowd watching on a big screen roared as the Knicks rallied from a 16-point deficit to beat the Spurs in San Antonio in Game 5 of the NBA Finals.

video:video.video:title: Emergency flood warning issued as Tropical Storm Jangmi makes landfall in western Japan
video:video.video:description: A tropical storm is dumping heavy rain and raising flood risks around east-central Japan as it moved into the heavily populated Tokyo region.

video:video.video:title: King Charles III is expected to reveal his personal tax bill in a bid to boost transparency
video:video.video:description: Britain's King Charles is expected to reveal his personal tax bill on Thursday, becoming the first British monarch to do so.

video:video.video:title: Democrat Xavier Becerra says he overcame underdog status in race for governor

video:video.video:description: The demonstrations came after a far-right citizens’ initiative seeking sweeping measures against migrants garnered enough support to be brought to Parliament.

video:video.video:title: Parts of Mexico with cartel violence celebrate World Cup differently
video:video.video:description: Much of Mexico has been celebrating the World Cup with joyful outdoor gatherings, filling streets, plazas and fan zones in the country’s three host cities — Mexico City, Guadalajara and Monterrey — since the tournament kickoff on June 11.

video:video.video:title: Four capybaras born in Jerusalem’s Biblical Zoo named after the Beatles
video:video.video:description: Four Capybara pups born in Jerusalem’s Biblical Zoo were named after the Beatles, namely John, Paul, George and Ringo. (AP video: Shlomo Mor)

video:video.video:title: Is Milly Alcock's 'Supergirl' LGBTQ+?
video:video.video:description: In a tribute to Pride Month, Milly Alcock ponders whether Kara Zor-El - aka Supergirl - identifies as LGBTQ+, as she attends a fan event for the new movie in London.

video:video.video:title: Iran's national team prepares for World Cup as war continues
video:video.video:description: Iran's national football team intensified their preparations on Wednesday ahead of the FIFA World Cup, holding an energetic training session in Antalya, Turkey as excitement builds across their country ahead of the tournament.

video:video.video:title: Soccer great Pelé's legacy shines on in his one-time home ahead of the World Cup
video:video.video:description: As the 2026 FIFA World Cup approaches, the image of one of Brazil's most recognizable soccer players lives on in Santos, the one-time home of Edson Arantes do Nascimento, far better known as Pelé.

video:video.video:title: Thousands of women march for justice for sexual violence victims in Nagaland
video:video.video:description: The small mountainous state of Nagaland, which has consistently ranked as one of the safest places for women in India, has been shaken by a series of sexual assaults in recent months.

video:video.video:title: What we know about a possible deal to end the Iran war
video:video.video:description: The apparent breakthrough in negotiations comes after Iran exchanged fire with the U.S. and Israel over three days this week, threatening to push the region into a full-scale war.

video:video.video:title: Landmarks and businesses are preparing for the World Cup's economic impact in Atlanta
video:video.video:description: Atlanta is not only welcoming soccer fans during the World Cup, but also business opportunities. Georgia is building the biggest fan zone the city has seen since the Olympics, landmarks are investing in soccer-themed souvenirs, and small businesses are finding new ways to expand.

video:video.video:title: Super fan honors African leader's legacy at World Cup
video:video.video:description: Congo’s famous living statue finally made his World Cup debut on Tuesday. Michel Nkuka Mboladinga, who gained fame during the Africa Cup of Nations for posing as a statue of Congo’s assassinated independence leader Patrice Lumumba for the entirety of games, attended Congo’s 1-0 loss to Colombia

video:video.video:title: Keir Starmer announces his resignation as Prime Minister and Leader of the Labour Party
video:video.video:description: Keir Starmer says he'll step down as UK Labour Party leader, will remain UK prime minister until successor chosen. (AP video by Henry Wilkins)

video:video.video:title: Mamdani slate sweeps Democratic primaries in New York, ousts 2 incumbents from Congress
video:video.video:description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s slate of fiery progressives swept establishment-backed Democrats in the state's congressional primaries on Tuesday, ousting two sitting congressmen.

video:video.video:title: Wall of nametags at South Korean park captures adoptees’ longing for their birth mothers
video:video.video:description: Dozens of Korean adoptees from North America and Europe recently gathered to leave their names on a wall at a former U.S. military base, hoping that, after decades, a birth mother might still be looking for them.

video:video.video:title: Hundreds protest outside Kenya's Ebola quarantine center for US citizens
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of youths in Kenya’s central town of Nanyuki on Monday demonstrated against the establishment at the Laikipia Air Base of an Ebola quarantine center for American citizens exposed to the virus.

video:video.video:title: Pakistan's delegation arrives in Obbuergen for high-level talks between the United States and Iran
video:video.video:description: Pakistan’s Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and his delegation arrived in Switzerland on Sunday to participate in high-level talks between the United States and Iran on the implementation of the Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding being held in Switzerland.

video:video.video:title: Putin defends state of Russian economy in meeting with international media
video:video.video:description: President Vladimir Putin said on Thursday claims the Russian economy was struggling amid the ongoing military operation in Ukraine were "wishful thinking".

video:video.video:title: Israeli strikes on Tyre leave homes, infrastructure and ancient site in ruins
video:video.video:description: The port city of Tyre in southern Lebanon has been targeted by near-daily Israeli airstrikes that have leveled entire buildings and forced many residents to flee to safer areas elsewhere in Lebanon.

video:video.video:title: US Central Command releases video of strikes on Iran in second day of attacks
video:video.video:description: U.S. Central Command released video early Sunday purporting to show strikes on Iranian military targets.

video:video.video:title: Dutch fans flood Kansas City with orange as the ‘Oranjebus’ makes its way downtown
video:video.video:description: The Oranjebus made its way through downtown Kansas City toward the FIFA Fan Festival ahead of a World Cup match between the Netherlands and Tunisia.

video:video.video:title: No respite from intense heatwave for outdoor laborers in Lahore
video:video.video:description: Lahore is experiencing an intense heatwave with temperatures expected to reach up to 47 degrees Celsius (117 degrees Fahrenheit) this week.

video:video.video:title: Two children, one aged four, killed in Israeli attack on central Gaza
video:video.video:description: A four-year-old and a 14-year-old have died after an Israeli strike hit an apartment in the central Gaza Strip at around 2 a.m local time Saturday.

video:video.video:title: Five killed in explosion and fire at defense company in Daejeon, South Korea
video:video.video:description: An explosion and fire occurred at a defense company in South Korea on Monday, killing five people and injuring two others, officials said.

video:video.video:title: Daniel Radcliffe has no plans to stop doing theater

video:video.video:title: Austrian drag queens brave record temperatures at annual drag sports event
video:video.video:description: Athletes on Saturday braved record temperatures at a drag queen spoof sporting event in Vienna. Temperatures in the Austrian capital soared past 38 degrees Celsius (100.4 Fahenheit) breaking an earlier record from 2012.

video:video.video:description: Despite the Ebola outbreak in Bunia, in the Democratic Republic of Congo, some families were determined to go ahead with their relatives’ weddings.

video:video.video:title: G7 leaders to discuss Ukraine on first full day of meetings at summit
video:video.video:description: Leaders of the Group of Seven are discussing Russia's war in Ukraine as they meet for the first full day at the G7 summit of leading industrialized nations on Tuesday in the French town of Evian-les-Bains.

video:video.video:title: San Francisco not expecting big influx of tourists for World Cup games
video:video.video:description: The San Francisco Bay Area is preparing to host six World Cup games, but tourism officials don’t expect a big influx of international visitors due to geopolitical issues.

video:video.video:title: Israeli warplanes strike Nabatiyeh in south Lebanon as troops advance in nearby villages
video:video.video:description: Heavy smoke was seen rising from an Israeli airstrike at the entrance of the city of Nabatiyeh. Israel's military appears to be trying to make as many gains as possible in case a U.S.

video:video.video:title: U.S. and Iranian officials arrive in Switzerland to launch talks on Tehran’s nuclear program
video:video.video:description: U.S. Vice President JD Vance and senior Iranian officials arrived in Switzerland on Sunday to formally launch negotiations over Tehran’s nuclear program, build out the fragile interim deal to end the war in Iran and keep the Strait of Hormuz open.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he hasn't been briefed on alleged plot against White House UFC event
video:video.video:description: Speaking on the sidelines of the G7 summit, President Donald Trump said he had not been briefed on an alleged plot targeting the UFC event at the White House on Sunday.

video:video.video:title: UN forces clear rubble from Lebanese village of Dibbine after Israeli troop withdrawal
video:video.video:description: The United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon on Friday removed rubble and cleared the village of Dibbine, the first area from which Israeli troops withdrew from Lebanon.

video:video.video:title: Iranians celebrate World Cup draw but must wait to see if team progresses
video:video.video:description: Young supporters celebrated in Tehran early on Saturday after Iran drew 1-1 against Egypt in the World Cup tournament as Team Melli will have to wait a day to find out if they will make it to the next round of the soccer tournament after the Iranians scored a potential late winning goal, but an offs

video:video.video:title: Gov. Greg Abbott lays out plans to combat a New World screwworm infestation in Texas
video:video.video:description: Texas Gov. Greg Abbott lays out plans to combat a New World screwworm infestation in Texas, emphasizing that there is a well-established prevention and cure for the spread that the federal government has implemented in the past.

video:video.video:title: Ukrainian President writes direct letter to Putin calling for negotiations to end the war
video:video.video:description: The letter is the first public message Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has written directly to Russian President Vladimir Putin since Russia launched its full-scale invasion in 2022.

video:video.video:title: WHO chief honors four nurses who survived Ebola in DR Congo
video:video.video:description: The Director General of the World Health Organisation, Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, presented four nurses with certificates on Sunday confirming that they had recovered from Ebola in Bunia, eastern DRC.

video:video.video:title: Colorful displays show World Cup anticipation has reached city in Brazil's Amazon rainforest
video:video.video:description: As countries prepare for the FIFA World Cup, anticipation has reached fever pitch in a city in the heart of the Brazilian rainforest. (AP Video by Fernando Crispim)

video:video.video:title: AP talks to Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang
video:video.video:description: The Associated Press gives its takeaways after an interview with Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang on everything from AI to President Donald Trump.

video:video.video:title: US stocks jump to their best day in 2 months on hopes for a deal to get crude flowing globally again
video:video.video:description: U.S. stocks rallied to their best day in two months, and oil prices fell Thursday after President Donald Trump called off his threat to bomb Iran in the evening.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo gets welcomed by a human tower in Barcelona
video:video.video:description: Human towers, or “castells” in the Catalan language, are not only a feat of equilibrium, strength and teamwork, but a crucial part of the proud identity in this northeastern Spanish region.

video:video.video:title: Dozens of children separated under first Trump administration have faced separation again
video:video.video:description: An Associated Press investigation reveals that dozens of children who were separated under the first Trump administration have been re-separated, despite a judge's order to reunite them.

video:video.video:title: Virginia man gets life in prison for double murder scheme in affair with Brazilian au pair
video:video.video:description: Brendan Banfield who was having an affair with the family’s Brazilian au pair was sentenced Friday to life in prison without parole for the murder of his wife and a man who was lured to the couple's home as a fall guy.

video:video.video:description: Stars Emilia Clarke and Haley Lu Richardson discuss the difficulties of speaking Russian and donning 1970s clothes to play widows-turned-spies in hit series "PONIES."

video:video.video:title: Tourists disappointed over closure of Versailles Palace amid preparations for Trump-Macron dinner
video:video.video:description: Hours before US President Donald Trump and French President Emmanuel Macron were scheduled to meet for dinner at the Palace of Versailles tourists unaware of the event were turned away at its closed gates, unable to visit.

video:video.video:title: The top photos of the week by Associated Press Photojournalists

video:video.video:title: How people celebrated midsummer 500 years before Stonehenge was built
video:video.video:description: A prehistoric site that could have been an early prototype for Stonehenge has been discovered by archaeologists in England.

video:video.video:title: Activists and conservationists protest in Nairobi over parking facility plans for national park
video:video.video:description: Environmental activists and conservationists staged a protest in Nairobi on Monday against plans to construct a parking facility inside Nairobi National Park.

video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV participated in the reading of the rosary at an ancient mountain-top abbey in Montserrat on Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: US strike on an alleged drug boat kills 3 in the eastern Pacific Ocean in fourth attack this week
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military said it carried out another strike Saturday on a boat accused of smuggling drugs in the eastern Pacific Ocean, killing three men in the fourth attack this week.

video:video.video:title: A new exchange of fire with Iran in the Gulf tests the fragile ceasefire
video:video.video:description: The latest exchange of fire came as the Trump administration pressed Iran to make a deal to end the war that has strained the global economy and threatened a hunger crisis in some of the world’s most vulnerable countries.

video:video.video:title: Starmer challenger Andy Burnham sworn in as UK lawmaker
video:video.video:description: Andy Burnham, who won a special parliamentary election last week, was sworn in as a lawmaker in the UK Parliament on Monday.

video:video.video:title: People living in attic apartments under zinc roofs of Paris suffer in the heat
video:video.video:description: Millions of people across Europe were exposed to extreme and exceptional high temperatures on Tuesday, with 40 fatalities from drowning recorded in France in the past week as residents seek relief from the searing heat.

video:video.video:title: Meet the team tailoring spacesuits for lunar astronauts
video:video.video:description: If all goes as planned, NASA is planning to send astronauts back to the moon in 2028. Artemis IV will be the first time humans have returned to the lunar surface since Apollo 17 in 1972.

video:video.video:title: NBA Draft prospects describe their outfits in three words
video:video.video:description: 2026 NBA Draft prospects talk fashion with AP!

video:video.video:title: Japanese royal family visits Belgian royal family at the Castle of Laeken
video:video.video:description: The Emperor and Empress of Japan were welcomed by the Belgian royal family at the Royal Castle of Laeken in Brussels on Monday. (AP Video by Mark Carlson)

video:video.video:title: Plane crash in Missouri leaves 12 dead, authorities say
video:video.video:description: Authorities say 12 people were killed when a plane crashed in Missouri. The Missouri State Highway Patrol said in a statement that troopers were on the scene, assisting the Butler Police Department & Bates County Sheriff’s Office.

video:video.video:title: Economic woes fuel discontent with Brexit in UK, decade after vote to leave EU
video:video.video:description: Some business leaders express frustration and discontent a decade after Britain voted to leave the European Union in 2016.

video:video.video:title: Serbia’s populist President Vucic says he will resign within weeks as election nears
video:video.video:description: Serbia's populist President Aleksandar Vucic said Saturday he will resign the post within weeks, apparently to pave the way for early elections following youth-led protests.

video:video.video:title: Fear grows ahead of Peru's runoff election as extortion and killings increase
video:video.video:description: Voters in Peru say violence and extortions are their main concerns as they head to the ballot box to elect a new president in a runoff elections.

video:video.video:title: Vance defends unwritten ‘gentlemen’s agreements’ with Iran
video:video.video:description: Vice President JD Vance defended the existence of a “gentlemen’s agreements” with Iran about how it handles its uranium stockpiles and nuclear ambitions.

video:video.video:title: Trump says Pulte won’t be the ‘permanent’ director of national intelligence
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump says Bill Pulte’s appointment as acting director of national intelligence would only be temporary, praising him as “a very smart guy” while saying the White House is actively interviewing candidates for the Senate-confirmed role.

video:video.video:title: Powerful earthquake hits Venezuela, swaying buildings in the capital
video:video.video:description: A powerful 7.1-magnitude earthquake shook Venezuela on Wednesday evening, collapsing buildings in the capital of Caracas. (AP/ Juan Arraez)

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV celebrates art, culture and sport at Madrid cultural event
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV watched a twirling Flamenco dancer and received a badminton racket from two athletes at an event in Madrid honoring art, culture and sport.

video:video.video:description: The milestone comes as climate change disrupts traditional olive-growing regions across the Mediterranean, driving up prices as demand for premium olive oil continues to grow.

video:video.video:description: When Brazilian João Victor Gonçalves began playing soccer in one of Rio de Janeiro’s poorest and most violent favelas, little did he know the game would one day allow him to travel abroad and play in an international competition.

video:video.video:description: Colin Farrell says making season two of "Sugar" often felt like "building the plane mid-flight," revealing that scripts were still being fleshed out as filming continued.

video:video.video:title: Trump's name to be removed from Kennedy Center after judge denies last-minute move to keep it
video:video.video:description: A judge cleared the way for President Donald Trump’s name to be removed from the Kennedy Center’s exterior on Friday, denying a last-minute request from the institution’s board that would have kept the name in place.

video:video.video:title: Tornado leaves trail of destruction in Merrillville, Indiana
video:video.video:description: Officials in Merrillville, Indiana said more than 200 buildings were damaged after a strong line of storms barreled through communities south of Chicago on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Lebanon considers its largest amnesty in 35 years but some are protesting
video:video.video:description: Lebanon's largest amnesty since the end of its devastating 1975-90 civil war is expected to occur in the coming weeks, once parliament approves it and the president signs it.

video:video.video:description: A fire at a massive warehouse blackened the skies near downtown Los Angeles on Wednesday afternoon, prompting shelter-in-place orders in the area due to the risk of hazardous air.

video:video.video:title: Video shows moment truck driver helps thwart alleged kidnapping
video:video.video:description: Footage from a truck-driving preacher's dashboard camera captured what appears to be a kidnapping attempt. Anthony J.

video:video.video:description: Nvidia unveiled new powerful chips on Monday that would bring advanced artificial intelligence functions into laptops and desktop computers.

video:video.video:description: Haitians are feeling mixed emotions as their team faces Brazil in the World Cup. Fans have long supported Brazil, drawn by cultural similarities and shared history.

video:video.video:description: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz on Tuesday gave U.S. President Donald Trump a soccer jersey with his name and the number 47 on the back.

video:video.video:title: Survivor saw floors 'going up and down' as powerful earthquake hit in southern Philippines
video:video.video:description: Residents in General Santos assessed the damage to their homes and buildings on Tuesday a day after a powerful earthquake struck the southern Philippines.

video:video.video:title: Rex Heuermann to be sentenced in New York’s Gilgo Beach serial killings
video:video.video:description: Sentencing for a Long Island architect who lived a secret life as the Gilgo Beach serial killer in the deaths of eight women is expected Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Scores of people rescued from deadly building fire in New Delhi
video:video.video:description: A fire swept through a building in a New Delhi neighborhood Wednesday, killing at least 18 people and injuring several others, news agency Press Trust of India reported.

video:video.video:description: Much of Mexico has been celebrating the World Cup with joyful outdoor gatherings, filling streets, plazas and fan zones in the country's three host cities — Mexico City, Guadalajara and Monterrey — since the tournament kickoff on June 11.

video:video.video:title: Ian McKellen honors home of Laurence Olivier
video:video.video:description: One British acting great, Ian McKellen, unveils a blue plaque on the childhood home of another, Sir Laurence Olivier, at 22 Lupus Street in Pimlico, London.

video:video.video:title: Argentina fans celebrate Messi-inspired win over Algeria
video:video.video:description: Thousands of Argentina supporters gathered at a Fan Zone in Buenos Aires to watch the defending champions defeat Algeria 3-0 in its opening match of the World Cup.

video:video.video:description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi on Sunday insisted his country would oversee the reopening and continued operation of the Strait of Hormuz.
