video:video.video:description: Fans of the Germany and Ecuador World Cup teams filled New York City’s Times Square on Wednesday, ahead of Thursday’s World Cup match.

video:video.video:title: CCTV footage shows moment magnitude 7.8 earthquake hit the Philippines
video:video.video:description: An offshore magnitude 7.8 earthquake rocked the southern Philippines Monday, killing at least 19 people, injuring more than 200 others mostly in damaged buildings and sending a three-foot tsunami into nearby coasts.

video:video.video:title: S3 of 'House of the Dragon' takes off
video:video.video:description: Stars promise the biggest and most epic season yet as "House of the Dragon" returns for season 3.

video:video.video:title: Shark fatally mauls spearfishing diver off Australia's Rottnest Island, police say
video:video.video:description: A shark fatally mauled a diver off an Australian tourist island on Saturday, police said.

video:video.video:title: Vance, skeptical of foreign wars, becomes the face of Trump's tentative deal to end war with Iran
video:video.video:description: Vice President JD Vance has embraced the role of being the chief defender of the agreement he and President Donald Trump signed with Iran over the weekend.

video:video.video:title: Pope names Mexican-American broadcasting executive to lead Vatican communications
video:video.video:description: Maria Montserrat Alvarado replaces Paolo Ruffini as prefect of the Dicastery of Communications, the office that controls the Vatican’s television, radio, online, publishing and newspaper operations.

video:video.video:title: 7.8 magnitude quake in Philippines kills at least 32, fells buildings and sets off tsunami
video:video.video:description: An offshore magnitude 7.8 earthquake rocked the southern Philippines on Monday, killing at least 32 people, injuring more than 200 others mostly in damaged buildings and sending a 1-meter (3-foot) tsunami into nearby coasts.

video:video.video:title: Family of infant shot by Mississippi police seeks independent autopsy
video:video.video:description: The family of a 1-year-old boy killed by police in Mississippi who were responding to a shoplifting report is calling on authorities to release video of the shooting.

video:video.video:title: Ukrainian cities of Dnipro and Kharkiv hit in overnight Russian drone and missile strikes
video:video.video:description: Russia attacked Ukraine with a barrage of missiles and drones overnight, killing at least 11 people, injuring dozens and trapping others, authorities said on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Trump ‘not happy’ with Israel’s handling of Hezbollah and Lebanon
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump on Tuesday said he is “not happy with the way Israel has handled themselves with Lebanon and with Hezbollah.”

video:video.video:title: Fisherman catches white shark, removes hook and releases the top predator fish off Massachusetts
video:video.video:description: A fisherman in Massachusetts reeled in and released a nine-foot white shark. Elliot Sudal, a veteran angler and boat captain, caught the shark – also known as a great white shark or a great white – on Nantucket.

video:video.video:title: Fear shadows Peru’s runoff vote as extortion and killings surge nationwide
video:video.video:description: Peru is grappling with a surge in extortion and violence, especially in areas like Trujillo. Gladys Saavedra, who works in a market there, says extortionists demand $300 monthly from vendors.

video:video.video:title: Argentina fans react after Messi sets World Cup scoring record
video:video.video:description: Lionel Messi set a World Cup record with his 17th and 18th goals, and defending champion Argentina advanced to the knockout stage with a 2-0 victory over Austria on Monday.

video:video.video:title: Palestinians turn to charity kitchens for food as shortages continue to grip the enclave
video:video.video:description: Palestinians crowded around a charity kitchen in northern Gaza on Monday, seeking food as shortages continued to grip the enclave.

video:video.video:title: Stadium workers near LA say they have a tentative deal, averting strike ahead of World Cup
video:video.video:description: Workers represented by UNITE HERE Local 11 said they were seeking pay increases, protections from subcontracting and security on the job amid ramped-up immigration enforcement under President Donald Trump’s administration.

video:video.video:title: 6 people hurt in stabbings at New York's Penn Station with a suspect in custody, authorities says
video:video.video:description: Authorities say six people were injured in a stabbing inside New York’s Penn Station Sunday evening. A suspect was taken into custody.

video:video.video:title: North Korean leader Kim emphasizes nuclear weapons need during Workers' Party meeting
video:video.video:description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un has stressed his need for nuclear weapons at a meeting of his ruling Workers' Party, state media reported early Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Matt Damon raps to fight global water crisis
video:video.video:description: Matt Damon discusses his mission to bring clean and accessible water to communities around the world with the Get Blue project. (June 11)

video:video.video:title: Polls open in Lima as Peruvians choose between two presidential hopefuls with very different views
video:video.video:description: Peruvians cast their ballots on Sunday in an election that will allow voters to choose their ninth president in just 10 years, either the conservative daughter of a disgraced former president or a nationalist congressman.

video:video.video:title: Founder of global gaming company Ubisoft killed in plane crash in France
video:video.video:description: Claude Guillemot, one of the founders of the global gaming company Ubisoft, the maker of Assassin’s Creed, and his flight instructor have been killed in a plane crash in western France, authorities said Saturday.

video:video.video:title: Pope reiterates support for quake-hit Venezuela, prays for victims
video:video.video:description: Pope reiterates support for quake-hit Venezuela, prays for victims

video:video.video:title: Vespa drivers join Rome parade as iconic scooter turns 80
video:video.video:description: Thousands of Vespas buzzed through Rome on Saturday, celebrating the scooter's 80th anniversary. Enthusiasts from Europe, the US, Australia and the Philippines gathered to honor the iconic brand.

video:video.video:title: Stocks leap worldwide, oil prices drop after the US and Iran reach a tentative deal to end war
video:video.video:description: Stock rallied worldwide, and oil prices eased after the United States and Iran reached a tentative deal to get the global flow of crude going again.

video:video.video:title: Iconic mountain rescue dogs keep history alive as museum celebrates first birthday
video:video.video:description: At the millennia-old Great St Bernard Pass, the famous dogs still walk the same mountain paths their ancestors patrolled for more than three centuries.

video:video.video:title: Back-to-back powerful earthquakes slam Venezuela, collapsing buildings in the capital of Caracas
video:video.video:description: Back-to-back powerful earthquakes slammed Venezuela on Wednesday evening, collapsing buildings in the capital of Caracas and leaving residents shaken.

video:video.video:title: Guinness crowns Canberra town crier as the world's loudest person at 122.4 decibels
video:video.video:description: Joseph McGrail-Bateup, an Australian professional air conditioner cleaner and honorary town crier, has been recognized as the world’s loudest person.

video:video.video:title: Steve Hilton recalls Arnold Schwarzenegger's California governorship in speech
video:video.video:description: Republican Steve Hilton, a conservative commentator and former British political adviser, said he would give the liberal state a reset after years of one-party rule.

video:video.video:title: Colombians react to runoff election as Trump-endorsed de la Espriella holds slim lead
video:video.video:description: Colombians reflected on the country's polarized presidential runoff in which conservative political outsider Abelardo de la Espriella held a narrow lead on Monday, with almost all votes counted.

video:video.video:title: Supreme Court OKs state laws allowing late-arriving mailed ballots to be counted
video:video.video:description: The Supreme Court has ruled that states can count mailed ballots that arrive after Election Day, preserving rules in nearly 30 states, and handing President Donald Trump a significant defeat.

video:video.video:title: Scuffles break out in Inglewood after Iran vs Belgium World Cup match
video:video.video:description: Scuffles break out on the street as Iran plays Belgium in the World Cup

video:video.video:title: What to look forward to in the NBA finals
video:video.video:description: Game one of the NBA Finals is Wednesday, and AP reporter Tim Reynolds is there. Here's what to expect.

video:video.video:title: South African inmates embrace art in prison to curb repeat offending
video:video.video:description: When most people think of prison, they picture steel bars, locked gates and lost freedom. But at a correctional facility in Johannesburg, South Africa’s largest city, visitors are greeted by something unexpected: an art gallery.

video:video.video:title: One dead and another injured after train strikes semi-truck in Iowa
video:video.video:description: A train struck a semi-truck in eastern Iowa on Wednesday, killing one person and injuring another, authorities said. (AP Video by Charlie Niebergall)

video:video.video:title: Bridge jump daredevils seek relief from heat in Paris
video:video.video:description: Crowds of young people improvised a game of cat and mouse with local police, as they flocked to the Saint-Martin Canal in Paris on Friday for a diving contest off of a footbridge that authorities have desperately tried to close off.

video:video.video:title: National pride meets breathable mesh: A look at the design of World Cup uniforms
video:video.video:description: Heat, moisture and national pride - a Nike executive talks about what guided the company when making the 2026 World Cup shirts and uniforms. (AP Video by Claire Rush)

video:video.video:title: Starmer says Britain will ban under-16s from using a range of social media apps
video:video.video:description: Britain will ban under-16s from using a range of social media apps, Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Monday, calling it “a big moment for our country.”

video:video.video:title: Feeling the heat: How extreme temperatures could impact the World Cup
video:video.video:description: As the FIFA World Cup kicks off, concerns are emerging over the impact of extreme temperatures on both players and spectators.

video:video.video:title: Trump calls bill to address housing affordability ‘a yawn’ and says he doesn’t know if he’ll sign it
video:video.video:description: House Speaker Mike Johnson said over the weekend he would send the bill to the president on Monday anyway.

video:video.video:title: Smoke over southern Lebanese village as Israel continues its attacks against Hezbollah
video:video.video:description: Thick smoke billowed over the southern Lebanese village of Kfar Tebnit on Friday as Israel continues its attacks against Hezbollah.

video:video.video:title: Fans react to England beating Panama 2-0 to win World Cup group
video:video.video:description: Rain couldn't dampen the spirits of fans attending the England versus Panama match at the MetLife Stadium in New Jersey on Friday.

video:video.video:title: Buffalo named Donald Trump for his golden locks delights crowds in Bangladesh
video:video.video:description: The rare albino bull became a sensation when a farmer suggested his blond tuft of hair resembled the U.S. president.

video:video.video:title: Officials say eight people died in B-52 bomber crash at US Air Force base in Southern California
video:video.video:description: A B-52 bomber crashed shortly after takeoff at a U.S. Air Force base in Southern California’s Mojave Desert and burst into flames Monday, killing all eight people aboard, military officials said.

video:video.video:title: Suspect dead after Texas shooting kills 1 and injures at least 9 others
video:video.video:description: A shooter who opened fire in the West Texas city of Midland died Friday after a standoff with police following an attack that left one person dead and at least nine others injured, city officials said.

video:video.video:title: North Korean state TV shows leader Kim Jong Un welcoming Chinese President Xi in Pyongyang
video:video.video:description: North Korean state television on Tuesday broadcast footage of Chinese President Xi Jinping's visit to Pyongyang, which started on Monday.

video:video.video:title: Police rescue hundreds of cats from being eaten in Vietnam with bust of major animal theft ring
video:video.video:description: Police in Vietnam seized more than 400 cats in a major bust of a cat meat crime ring in Ho Chi Minh City, according to animal welfare groups and local media reports.

video:video.video:title: Chaos in Venezuelan airport after massive earthquakes
video:video.video:description: A pair of powerful earthquakes rocked Venezuela, tearing down buildings, closing the country’s main airport and sending panicked residents of the capital pouring into the streets.

video:video.video:title: Federal Reserve keeps interest rates unchanged, Chairman Warsh holds first news conference
video:video.video:description: The Federal Reserve kept its key rate unchanged Wednesday yet half the central bank’s policymakers said they could support a rate hike later this year.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories June 26
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Friday, June 26th: Crews in Venezuela search for earthquake survivors; Supreme Court ruling exposes hundreds of thousands to potential deportation; UN pauses the evacuation of ships through the Strait of Hormuz; U.S.

video:video.video:title: Coastal development project linked to Trump's son-in-law faces growing opposition in Albania
video:video.video:description: A massive coastal development project linked to Jared Kushner, the son-in-law of U.S. President Donald Trump, is facing growing opposition in Albania where Saturday marked the 14th consecutive day of protests in the capital, Tirana.

video:video.video:title: Putin joins ASEAN leaders for family photo before first talks of joint summit in Russia
video:video.video:description: Leaders of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations gathered on Thursday for a second day of talks in Russia as Moscow seeks to bolster business and other ties with the nations of the regional bloc.

video:video.video:title: Scientists create walking robot fish to study evolutionary leap
video:video.video:description: How did the first ancient fish leave the water and learn to walk on land millions of years ago? Scientists think they have finally found an answer, and they used a robot to prove it.

video:video.video:title: Venezuelans take search for the missing into their own hands as earthquake death toll climbs
video:video.video:description: Omar Guariato was celebrating his granddaughter's 5th birthday at home when the earthquakes began. As he sat in a camp for displaced people in the hard-hit La Guaira, he recalled the moment the house next door collapsed.

video:video.video:title: World Cup ref denied entry to US had 'earned right to officiate at highest level'
video:video.video:description: A World Cup referee from Somalia who was denied entry to the United States after arriving in Miami and subsequently cut from the tournament by FIFA was set to make history for his country.

video:video.video:title: Oz: Trump keeps getting checkups because 'he likes the results'
video:video.video:description: Dr. Mehmet Oz called President Donald Trump’s health “spectacular” and suggested the president keeps getting checkups because “he likes the results.”

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories June 11
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Thursday, June 11th: U.S. launches second round of airstrikes across Iran; Iran says Strait of Hormuz is closed; Tens of thousands displaced after Philippines earthquake; Knicks beat Spurs in Game Four of NBA Finals.

video:video.video:title: 8 people died in B-52 bomber crash at US Air Force base in Southern California, officials say
video:video.video:description: A B-52 bomber crashed shortly after takeoff at a U.S. Air Force base in Southern California’s Mojave Desert and burst into flames Monday, killing all eight people aboard, military officials said.

video:video.video:title: Russia strikes civilian infrastructure in various parts of Ukraine
video:video.video:description: At least one person was killed and three others injured from a Russian missile strike on civilian infrastructure in Ukraine’s Odesa region, local officials said on Monday.

video:video.video:title: Prada goes back to basics
video:video.video:description: Prada serves up fashion's version of pasta pomodoro at Milan Fashion Week, a return to basics with a very unadorned, distilled collection. (June 21)

video:video.video:title: Labour’s Andy Burnham wins a UK special election, setting up a likely push to oust Prime Minister Keir Starmer
video:video.video:description: Labour’s Andy Burnham, the current mayor of Greater Manchester, has won a special election for a seat in Parliament that puts him in a position to challenge embattled Prime Minister Keir Starmer.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories June 25
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Thursday June 25th: Two massive earthquakes in Venezuela; Senate Republicans reject war powers resolution; Trump holds campaign-style rally to kick off America’s 250 celebration; Ecuadorian and German World Cup fans pack New York’s Times Square.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he's 'the boss' on arrival for first meeting of final day at G7 summit
video:video.video:description: US President Donald Trump told other G7 leaders ″I’m the boss’’. That was his opening phrase as he walked in — late — to the first session of the day at the G7 summit.

video:video.video:title: Norway fans perform 'Viking row' in Times Square before World Cup match
video:video.video:description: Norwegian football fans converge at New York's iconic Times Square ahead of their second Group I clash against Senegal, which is set for Monday night.

video:video.video:title: Fight erupts in Georgian Parliament amid deepening political tensions
video:video.video:description: A fistfight erupted in the Georgian Parliament on Friday during Prime Minister Irakli Kobakhidze's annual address.

video:video.video:title: Europe's heatwave continues, across Romania, Serbia and Croatia
video:video.video:description: mperatures have soared across Europe over the weekend. Almost all of Romania was under a Red Warning for heat, the Romanian meteorology authority said on Monday with temperatures surpassing 40 degrees Celsius (104 Fahrenheit).

video:video.video:title: Lithgow gets candid about his Tony nomination
video:video.video:description: Tony nominee John Lithgow says he wasn't surprised to earn a nomination for his portrayal of Roald Dahl in "Giant," calling it the kind of role that attracts attention.

video:video.video:title: Man pleads guilty to killing a top Minnesota Democrat and her husband, avoids death penalty
video:video.video:description: U.S. Attorney David Rosen said seeking the death penalty was taken off the table once Vance Boelter agreed to plead guilty and serve multiple consecutive life sentences.

video:video.video:title: 'Name one proven fact': Putin asks for proof of allegations of Russian illegal activity in Europe
video:video.video:description: Russian President Vladimir Putin held a meeting Thursday with heads of foreign media news agencies on the sidelines of the St. Petersburg International Economic Forum.

video:video.video:title: G7 leaders likely to discuss Iran at summit in France
video:video.video:description: World leaders began gathering in a French spa town Monday for a summit of the Group of Seven club of powerful democracies with a new impetus following President Donald Trump's announcement of an agreement that he says will bring an end to the U.S. war against Iran.

video:video.video:title: Venezuela's acting president declares state of emergency following earthquakes
video:video.video:description: Powerful back-to-back earthquakes struck Venezuela on Wednesday evening, collapsing buildings and sending panicked residents into the streets as communities across the South American country sustained damage.

video:video.video:title: Shipwreck reveals Roman Empire's commercial ties with Hungary
video:video.video:description: Artefacts recovered from a Roman shipwreck found off the coast of present-day Croatia are giving historians new insights into ancient trade networks.

video:video.video:title: Venezuelans pull loved ones from rubble
video:video.video:description: Venezuelans took the search for missing loved ones into their own hands Friday in the aftermath of back-to-back earthquakes, citing the scarcity of government rescuers, as the human toll of the disaster climbed to at least 920 dead and more than 3,300 hurt.

video:video.video:title: Could Wimbledon's new AI app features replace sports commentators?
video:video.video:description: From AI generated match analysis to a chatbot that can answer questions about players and live matches, Wimbledon has been expanding the role of AI across its app and website.

video:video.video:title: UK's Trooping the Colour flypast captured in London skies
video:video.video:description: Aircraft from Britain's Royal Air Force were captured roaring through London skies on Saturday, as the King celebrated his official birthday with the Trooping of the Colour ceremony.

video:video.video:title: Students cry and hug each other after school shooting in the Philippines
video:video.video:description: Two young students opened fire in a high school in the central Philippines on Monday, killing three fellow students and wounding another seven, police said.

video:video.video:title: Dogs splash through pool as Mississippi animal shelter weathers extreme heat
video:video.video:description: Amid soaring temperatures, dogs at a Jackson, Mississippi animal shelter with no air conditioning cooled off in a splash pool Tuesday afternoon.

video:video.video:title: Priced out of World Cup games, Mexican fans take celebrations into their own hands
video:video.video:description: Priced out of stadium tickets to the very tournament their country is hosting alongside the U.S. and Canada, many Mexicans are reclaiming the event and staging their own celebrations on the streets.

video:video.video:title: Artist suing FIFA over destruction of Dallas whale mural before World Cup
video:video.video:description: The artist who painted a giant mural on a building in downtown Dallas of life-sized swimming whales has filed a $25 million lawsuit against soccer's international governing body and others, saying they illegally painted over his work to promote the city's upcoming World Cup matches.

video:video.video:title: Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass advances to a runoff in her bid for reelection
video:video.video:description: After a tough first term framed by the most destructive wildfire in city history and an ongoing struggle with widespread homelessness, Bass advanced to a November runoff Tuesday as she fights to stay in City Hall against challengers from both ends of the political spectrum.

video:video.video:title: Cape Verdeans celebrate as tiny nation reaches World Cup knockout round
video:video.video:description: Supporters danced and celebrated as the small island nation of Cape Verde completed an improbable run through the group stage with a third straight World Cup draw to become the smallest country to earn a spot in the knockout as it makes it's debut on soccer's grandest stage.

video:video.video:title: Survivors of first Ebola Bundibugyo strain in 2007 share how the town learned from their experience
video:video.video:description: Two decades ago, the first-ever outbreak of the Bundibugyo strain of Ebola emerged as an unfamiliar and alarming illness.

video:video.video:title: Incumbent who led Trump impeachment faces Mamdani-backed challenger in NY Democratic primary
video:video.video:description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani stopped Tuesday outside a polling site on the Lower East Side for an appearance with congressional candidate Brad Lander, a fellow Democrat.

video:video.video:title: Tokyo celebrates as Japan secures World Cup knockout spot
video:video.video:description: Soccer fans in Tokyo celebrated on Friday after Japan drew with Sweden in their final World Cup group stage match, sending both sides through to the knockout round.

video:video.video:title: Mexico City subway station goes viral over chandeliers
video:video.video:description: Mexico City's subway is known for its crowds and fast pace. But lately, one station has become famous for something unexpected: chandeliers.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo receives royal welcome as he kicks off his six-day visit to Spain
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV received a royal welcome from King Felipe and Queen Leticia as he kicked off a six-day visit to Spain. (AP video shot by Silvia Stellacci)

video:video.video:title: Hegseth invokes immigration and ‘invasion’ in D-Day speech in France
video:video.video:description: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth used a D-Day anniversary speech on Saturday to appear to link immigration by sea to the wartime liberation of Europe, warning that the freedom won by Allied troops could prove temporary if leaders failed to defend it.

video:video.video:title: Zelenskyy arrives in Geneva to attend the G7 summit
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy arrived in Geneva on Monday evening to join the leaders of the G7 countries.

video:video.video:title: Trump taps Bill Pulte as acting director of national intelligence to replace Tulsi Gabbard
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump has tapped Bill Pulte, head of the Federal Housing Finance Agency, to be the acting director of national intelligence.

video:video.video:title: 'The last straw': South African wheat farmers feel squeeze of Iran war
video:video.video:description: Farmers around the world are feeling the squeeze of the Iran war. Gas prices have shot up and fertilizer supplies have waned due to Tehran’s near shutdown of the Strait of Hormuz in retaliation for U.S. and Israeli bombing.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories June 12
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Friday, June 12th: Police say gunman dead in Texas mass shooting; Midwest residents grapple with tornado damage; Pakistani prime minister says U.S.- Iran deal is nearing; American and Canadian fans catch World Cup fever.

video:video.video:title: California Gov. Gavin Newsom says Trump's Justice Department is investigating him and his wife
video:video.video:description: Democratic California Gov. Gavin Newsom accused the Justice Department of launching a politically-motivated investigation into him and his wife, saying the Trump administration was targeting him as he weighs a presidential bid.

video:video.video:title: Why is the World Cup so long?
video:video.video:description: We're a few weeks into the FIFA World Cup and you're probably asking, "Why is the World Cup so long?" Here's the answer.

video:video.video:title: A huge Viking textile production site is unearthed in Denmark
video:video.video:description: Archaeologists say they have unearthed a “huge” Viking Age textile production site in Danish Jutland.

video:video.video:title: Spies and lies with "The Agency" stars
video:video.video:description: Trust no one? Well maybe not those who lie for a living, say Katherine Waterston and Michael Fassbender, stars of spy drama "The Agency."

video:video.video:title: Pentagon chief lashes out at NATO allies and announces review of US forces in Europe
video:video.video:description: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth lashed out at NATO allies on Thursday, announcing a six-month Pentagon review of American forces in Europe that will depend on how fast they take responsibility for their own security.

video:video.video:title: House approves war powers resolution to halt military action against Iran
video:video.video:description: Wednesday's action defied President Donald Trump as a handful of Republicans joined with Democrats to end the three-month-long war.

video:video.video:title: Relatives mourn Palestinian victims of overnight Israeli airstrikes on Gaza City
video:video.video:description: Nine Palestinians died in at least four separate Israeli strikes in Gaza City, according to Shifa Hospital, which received the bodies.

video:video.video:title: Native resilience shines through as US marks 250th
video:video.video:description: More than fireworks and apple pie, America’s 250th birthday marks a milestone of resilience for Native Americans.

video:video.video:title: Missing persons posters mimic World Cup stickers in Guadalajara
video:video.video:description: The faces of Mexico's missing are appearing in a familiar format usually reserved for soccer stars, as families adopt the look of World Cup collectible stickers to keep attention on the country's disappearance crisis.

video:video.video:title: Inde Navarette says working on 'Obsession' unlocked a newfound confidence within herself
video:video.video:description: Inde Navarrette says working on "Obsession" gave her a newfound confidence in herself. Navarrette's career has skyrocketed her to global fame and established her as a fan-favorite scream queen.

video:video.video:title: Iran and US exchange fire in Persian Gulf putting nominal ceasefire under further strain
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military said Tuesday that Iran fired missiles at Kuwait and Bahrain that failed or were shot down, and that the U.S. launched strikes on an Iranian facility in response.

video:video.video:title: Accra erupts with excitement as Ghana holds England scoreless, despite flurry-filled final minutes
video:video.video:description: Soccer fans gathered in the Ghanaian capital, Accra on Tuesday to watch their national team draw 0-0 with England in the World Cup.

video:video.video:title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says Russia has 'once again chosen war'
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy on Friday said Russia "has once again chosen war." Russian leader Vladimir Putin rejected a proposal by Zelenskyy for a face-to-face meeting on ending the 4-year-old conflict, saying he sees “no point” in it.

video:video.video:title: Traders face big losses after Uganda closes Congo border over Ebola contagion fears
video:video.video:description: Traders are facing big losses after Uganda closed its border with the Democratic Republic of the Congo over Ebola contagion fears. (AP Video by Patrick Onen)

video:video.video:title: Canadian PM says officials working through issues after Canada-US bridge opening delay
video:video.video:description: The opening of a Canadian-U.S. bridge across the Detroit River, which President Donald Trump had previously threatened to block, has been delayed due to unresolved issues.

video:video.video:title: Spanish Catholic church reckons with long-standing cases of sexual abuse
video:video.video:description: Spain’s long-delayed reckoning with sexual abuse within the Catholic Church entered a new phase this year with the launch of a reparations program for cases in which accused clergy members have died and whose alleged crimes are too old to be prosecuted.

video:video.video:title: Vietnamese police rescue hundreds of cats from being eaten with bust of major animal theft ring
video:video.video:description: More than 40 of the rescued cats were reunited with their owners following the multiday operation last week, but several dozen of those rescued have died due to the harsh conditions that they were found in, the groups said.

video:video.video:title: An ancient oak tree said to have sheltered legendary Robin Hood has died
video:video.video:description: The 1,200-year-old Major Oak in Sherwood Forest, linked to the legend of Robin Hood, is believed to have died. (AP Production: Marissa Duhaney)

video:video.video:title: Iran and US reach an initial deal to end the war and open the Strait of Hormuz but challenges remain
video:video.video:description: The United States and Iran on Monday reached an initial agreement that would extend their shaky ceasefire and lead to the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Video shows panic in immediate aftermath of double earthquake in Venezuela's capital Caracas
video:video.video:description: Footage showed panic in the streets of Caracas as back-to-back earthquakes struck the Venezuelan capital.

video:video.video:title: Pont Neuf Cavern by artist JR opens to visitors in Paris
video:video.video:description: One of the most ambitious public artworks Paris has seen in decades - the Pont Neuf Cavern by artist JR - opened to the public on Monday evening.

video:video.video:title: Police say 5 people have died in a shooting in Stade in northern Germany
video:video.video:description: Police said the shooting took place in the facility on Dankersstrasse, a street south of the town center. They said that there was no danger to the public.

video:video.video:title: Who is Andy Burnham, the lawmaker seeking to replace Keir Starmer?
video:video.video:description: The 56-year-old politician presents himself as an amiable northern everyman who prefers T-shirts to a suit and tie and spends spare time playing soccer or spinning 1990s tunes during DJ battles.

video:video.video:title: Emergency services in attendance after skydiving plane crashes in northeastern France, killing all 11 on board
video:video.video:description: Emergency services in attendance after skydiving plane crashes in northeastern France, killing all 11 on board

video:video.video:title: Thousands join Pride celebrations across Latin America
video:video.video:description: Members of the LGBTQ+ community across Latin America celebrated Pride on Saturday with parades, music and dancing.

video:video.video:title: Serbia's raspberry growers face climate change and low prices
video:video.video:description: Serbia is one of the world's biggest raspberry producers. In one town that relies on the fruit, climate change and unstable purchase prices are making it harder to do business.

video:video.video:title: Armed clashes erupt in Somalia capital ahead of planned anti-government rally
video:video.video:description: No official casualty figures were immediately available from the violence that prompted calls for restraint from the United Nations and the United States as the government and opposition traded blame for the violence.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV sits in cockpit for flight from Madrid to Barcelona
video:video.video:description: The pope appeared to be having a great time sitting in a jump seat between the two pilots, even talking to passengers on the intercom.

video:video.video:title: Trump warns Iran: ‘I’ll do what I have to do’ if deal is violated
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump warned Monday, "I will do what I have to do” if Iran fails to uphold it's peace deal with Washington.

video:video.video:title: 'The Bear' cast says goodbye to the show
video:video.video:description: Jeremy Allen White, Ayo Edebiri, Ebon Moss-Bachrach and Will Poulter talk about the personal impact of working on "The Bear" as they premiere the hit show's fifth and final season in New York.

video:video.video:title: Largest wildfire in the US burns through forests in Utah
video:video.video:description: The largest fire in the United States is burning its way though the mountains of Utah

video:video.video:title: Rahm Emanuel's New Hampshire bike tour tests 2028 presidential bid
video:video.video:description: Rahm Emanuel has been making moves in New Hampshire, hinting at a possible return to Washington politics, by biking more than 100 miles across the state, which typically holds the first presidential primary.

video:video.video:title: AI-powered radar helps avoid conflict with polar bears in Canada
video:video.video:description: Arctic sea ice is shrinking from climate change forcing many polar bears have to shift their diets to land during parts of the summer.

video:video.video:title: Police say 12 people dead and nine wounded in late-night shooting in South Africa
video:video.video:description: A group of armed attackers drove into a poor neighborhood in South Africa’s biggest city, got out of a minivan and opened fire at residents in a late-night attack in Johannesburg that killed 12 people and wounded at least nine, police said Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Colombian presidential candidates vote in runoff round results
video:video.video:description: Colombian presidential contenders voted in a closely fought presidential runoff Sunday between a progressive and a conservative outsider.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he 'might' stay in Europe for Iran deal signing, quips Vance gets the blame if it fails
video:video.video:description: Speaking at a news conference as the G7 comes to a close, President Donald Trump says he "might" stick around in Europe for the signing of his Iran deal in Switzerland and jokes that if the agreement is a failure, he’s blaming his Vice President JD Vance.

video:video.video:title: Russian strike kills two and injures 17 in Zaporizhzhia
video:video.video:description: Russian strike kills two and injures 17 in Zaporizhzhia

video:video.video:title: Hundreds march in Kathmandu for Nepal's Pride Month
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of members of Nepal's LGBTQ+ community and their supporters took to the streets of Kathmandu on Saturday to celebrate Pride Month.

video:video.video:title: Philadelphia Stadium ready for World Cup heat with cooling benches installed
video:video.video:description: Philadelphia’s World Cup stadium is ready for the summer heat, with cooling benches installed on the sidelines to help players cope with high temperatures expected during the tournament.

video:video.video:title: Sagrada Familia lights up as Pope Leo blesses the world's tallest church in Spain
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV has celebrated the Sagrada Familia Basilica as a masterpiece of “stones, colors and light.”

video:video.video:title: Javier Bardem unleashed in new 'Cape Fear'
video:video.video:description: Stars Javier Bardem, Amy Adams and Patrick Wilson explain how modern tech and family are at the heart of Apple TV's new 10-part series "Cape Fear," based on the 1962 and 1991 movies of the same name.

video:video.video:title: A ransom note about Nancy Guthrie's disappearance says she died, CNN reports
video:video.video:description: A ransom note about Nancy Guthrie's disappearance says she died, CNN reports

video:video.video:title: Allison Janney says acting helps her to build a legacy
video:video.video:description: Allison Janney says joining the world of "Minions & Monsters" is about more than just landing another role. (June 28)

video:video.video:title: Footage from a boat captures Blue Origin rocket exploding on the launch pad
video:video.video:description: A rocket belonging to Jeff Bezos’ Blue Origin exploded during a test at the launch pad, shaking nearby homes and briefly painting the sky orange.

video:video.video:title: Ike Barinholtz on playing Elon Musk: 'After spending time in that world, I want out'
video:video.video:description: Ike Barinholtz, who portrays Elon Musk in director Luca Guadagnino's upcoming film "Artificial," said that immersing himself in Musk's world left him eager to move on: "After spending time in that world, I want out."

video:video.video:title: Israeli and Lebanese officials confirm truce agreement after US-mediated talks in Washington
video:video.video:description: Ambassadors from Israel and Lebanon said the agreement includes the creation of a number of “pilot” security zones inside Lebanon from which Hezbollah militants would be banned.

video:video.video:title: Vance touts Trump's Iran deal as a 'win' that's 'already bearing real fruits'
video:video.video:description: Vice President JD Vance defended President Trump’s Iran deal as a "win” for Americans, citing lower gas prices and renewed shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: New trials ordered for two paramedics in the death of Elijah McClain
video:video.video:description: A Colorado court has ordered new trials for two paramedics found guilty in the death of Elijah McClain, almost seven years after the Black man was pinned down by police and injected with a fatal dose of ketamine.

video:video.video:title: Thousands visit University of Michigan arboretum for yearly peony peek
video:video.video:description: Visitors are making the annual visit to the University of Michigan to see and smell one of the world’s premier collections of herbaceous peonies.

video:video.video:title: New Orleans WWII Museum honors Rosie the Riveters for 82nd D-Day anniversary
video:video.video:description: For the 82nd anniversary of D-Day, New Orleans' World War II Museum features 30 Rosie the Riveters; women civilian war workers who took on demanding roles typically reserved for men during WWII.

video:video.video:title: Ebola fears drive pregnant women away from hospitals in DR Congo
video:video.video:description: Since the outbreak of the Ebola epidemic in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, pregnant women have begun to avoid hospitals for fear of becoming infected or being placed in quarantine.

video:video.video:title: Duet with Madonna for Barinholtz?
video:video.video:description: Ike Barinholtz discusses Madonna’s cameo in the upcoming second season of “The Studio."

video:video.video:title: Jack White: Musician turned artist
video:video.video:description: Musician Jack White explains why now is the perfect time for him to launch his debut art show in London.

video:video.video:title: How many people did you travel to the FIFA World Cup with?
video:video.video:description: From groups of 3 to 300, these fans share how many people they traveled to the FIFA World Cup with.

video:video.video:title: Javier Bardem cements Hollywood legacy
video:video.video:description: Academy Award-winner Javier Bardem thanks his wife Penélope Cruz as he creates prints of his hands, feet - and nose - at the TCL Chinese Theatre. (June 17)

video:video.video:title: Helicopters collide over Rio de Janeiro, killing 6
video:video.video:description: Two helicopters collided over Rio de Janeiro on Sunday morning and crashed in the city’s western zone, killing all six people aboard, firefighters said.

video:video.video:title: Los Angeles warehouse fire in Boyle Heights prompts shelter-in-place orders due to hazardous air
video:video.video:description: A fire at a massive warehouse blackened the skies near downtown Los Angeles on Wednesday afternoon, prompting shelter-in-place orders in the area due to the risk of hazardous air.

video:video.video:title: Putin says it is 'far too early' to talk about whether he will run for another term as president
video:video.video:description: Putin claimed he was yet to consider whether to run for another term that would keep him in office until 2036, when he'll be 83.

video:video.video:title: Peruvian shamans perform ritual of sound and smoke to bless candidates ahead of presidential runoff
video:video.video:description: Peruvian shamans gathered on Monday to perform a ritual for the two contenders vying for the country's presidency.

video:video.video:title: Man arrested on suspicion of collecting bones from graves in Hungary
video:video.video:description: Police in Hungary have arrested a 30-year-old man who investigators say collected human body parts that he'd gathered from abandoned cemeteries and from his workplace at a hospital.

video:video.video:title: World Cup to kick off in Mexico City as fans celebrate and protesters take to the streets
video:video.video:description: Mexico launches the 2026 World Cup before a global audience as President Claudia Sheinbaum faces a high-profile test as protesters take to the streets.

video:video.video:title: Donald Trump celebrates 80th birthday with UFC show on White House lawn
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump turned 80 on Sunday and celebrated with a unique event: cage fighting on the White House lawn.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories June 5
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Friday, June 5th: Senate votes to provide $70B in new funding for DHS; U.S. economy adds 172,000 jobs in May; Virginia man sentenced to life for double-murder scheme; Putin rebuffs Zelenskyy over face-to-face meeting.

video:video.video:title: Trump says war with Iran will end 'one way or the other' after criticizing Democrats
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump reiterated his confidence that the war in Iran will soon come to an end and gas prices will fall.

video:video.video:title: In Taylor Swift’s beach town, every clue becomes a wedding rumor
video:video.video:description: Rumors that Taylor Swift was planning to marry at her Rhode Island home this weekend have drawn fans, photographers and curious visitors to the seaside village of Watch Hill after a large tent appeared near her estate.

video:video.video:title: Thousands of Shiite Muslims commemorate Ashoura in the Iraqi holy city of Karbala
video:video.video:description: Shiite Muslims across the world marked Ashoura on Thursday, a holy day symbolizing sacrifice and martyrdom.

video:video.video:title: Colombia prepares to head to the polls during the runoff election on Sunday
video:video.video:description: On Sunday Colombian voters will decide between the status quo and a move to the right. (AP/ Samuel Sotomayor)

video:video.video:title: 'The Pont Neuf Cave' artwork opens
video:video.video:description: "La Caverne du Pont Neuf" ("The Pont Neuf Cave") by artist JR, opened to the Parisian public on Monday.

video:video.video:title: The scars of war among survivors in Sudan
video:video.video:description: Three years of war have devastated much of Sudan. The impact has been pressed into the skin of survivors, and their memories. Here are some of their stories.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories June 15
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Monday, June 15th: United States and Iran reach initial agreement; Trump heads to G7 summit in France; Ukraine says Russian attack hit centuries-old religious site; 11 skydivers dead in Missouri plane crash.

video:video.video:title: In Brazil’s Cerrado region, Indigenous fire practices reshape wildfire strategy
video:video.video:description: The Xerente people in northern Tocantins state have mastered controlled burning to manage the land. Their expertise is being incorporated into Brazil's public policy as authorities combine scientific research with traditional knowledge.

video:video.video:title: Animals at Rome zoo get cool refreshments to help them cope with high temperatures
video:video.video:description: Animals at a zoo in Rome were given frozen treats to help them cool off as the Italian capital sizzled in a scorching heatwave on Friday.

video:video.video:title: Crews prepare to remove Trump's name from Kennedy Center
video:video.video:description: Construction crews prepared to remove President Donald Trump’s name from the Kennedy Center Friday after a judge rejected a last-minute request from the board to keep it in place.

video:video.video:title: Sherpa guide missing on Everest for days rescued after crawling toward base camp
video:video.video:description: A Sherpa guide was found crawling to base camp on Mount Everest a week after he went missing, and was reunited with his family who had given up hope he would return.

video:video.video:title: Explosion seriously injures 3 in Monaco as the suspect flees into France
video:video.video:description: A blast from an explosive device has seriously injured three people at a residential building in Monaco, and the attacker fled to France, local authorities said.

video:video.video:title: US Defence Secretary Hegseth at D-Day ceremony in France
video:video.video:description: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth laid tributes at a memorial in France on Saturday as he attended a ceremony marking 82 years since the D-Day landings.

video:video.video:title: Merz gifts Trump German soccer jersey reading 'Trump 47' at G7 summit
video:video.video:description: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz on Tuesday gave U.S. President Donald Trump a soccer jersey with his name and the number 47 on the back.

video:video.video:title: Xi's trip to North Korea will be the latest step by China to reinforce its close ties with its neighbour
video:video.video:description: Chinese leader Xi Jinping will travel to North Korea next week, both countries have announced, in what will be his first visit in nearly seven years.

video:video.video:title: Two-day-old baby elephant born in UK zoo plays with ball on eve of World Cup
video:video.video:description: A baby Asian elephant made his debut at a UK zoo on Wednesday at just two days old. On the eve of the FIFA World Cup, the baby boy was given a ball to play with and could be seen attempting a few dribbles and investigating with his trunk.

video:video.video:title: With rising crime on their minds, Peruvians vote for president yet again
video:video.video:description: Peruvians took to the polls on Sunday to choose between two presidential hopefuls with starkly different views. (AP video shot by: Cesar Barreto)

video:video.video:title: Bodies of siblings pulled from rubble of apartment as Venezuela earthquake rescue efforts continue
video:video.video:description: Families in the Venezuelan city of La Guaira mourned loved ones and searched for survivors Thursday after a pair of powerful earthquakes brought down apartment buildings and left scores of people trapped beneath the rubble across the country.

video:video.video:title: No J.Lo, then no 'Office Romance' romcom says Brett Goldstein
video:video.video:description: Actor and writer Brett Goldstein says "Office Romance," his new romcom with Jennifer Lopez, was written with the pop icon in mind and would not have been created if she hadn't signed on.

video:video.video:title: Krys Marshall on filming 'Paradise' while pregnant
video:video.video:description: Here's what makes the second season of "Paradise" special — and deeply personal — for Krys Marshall.

video:video.video:title: Solar cold storage helps African farmers cut losses and reach global markets
video:video.video:description: Kenyan smallholder farmers are reducing post-harvest losses and accessing global export markets through a pay-per-use solar cold storage model pioneered by Nairobi-based company SoKo Fresh.

video:video.video:title: SpaceX goes public in historic Wall Street debut
video:video.video:description: SpaceX began trading on Wall Street on Friday in what is expected to be the largest stock market debut in history, a milestone that could make Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire.

video:video.video:title: North Korean leader Kim tours missile factory as his sister says no to denuclearisation
video:video.video:description: North Korean state media has shown leader Kim Jong Un visiting a missile production facility and calling for more than doubling the country's missile production capacity.

video:video.video:title: Jordan Chiles: 'Women can do anything'
video:video.video:description: Jordan Chiles talks about the importance of support for women's sports. She was nominated for sportswoman of the year at the BET Awards.

video:video.video:title: 'House of the Dragon' star Emma D'Arcy: It's time for a matriarchy
video:video.video:description: "House of the Dragon" star Emma D'Arcy says "Give a matriarchy a go."

video:video.video:title: 7.8 magnitude earthquake shakes part of southern Philippines
video:video.video:description: A magnitude 7.8 earthquake has shaken part of the southern Philippines, and a tsunami was possible on some regional coasts.

video:video.video:title: Armenia prepares for an election that could reshape ties with Moscow and the West
video:video.video:description: Armenia's parliamentary elections Sunday will focus on its geopolitical future as Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan seeks closer ties with the EU and the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Watch the moment powerful earthquakes rocked Venezuela
video:video.video:description: A pair of powerful earthquakes rocked Venezuela, tearing down buildings, closing the country’s main airport and sending panicked residents of the capital pouring into the streets.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories June 4
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Thursday, June 4th: U.S. consulate in Hong Kong commemorates Tiananmen anniversary with lit candles in windows; Nursing home fire kills at least 12 in Sri Lanka; Israeli drone strikes in Lebanon; Modi and Delcy Rodriguez meet in New Dehli.

video:video.video:title: Albania's prime minister vows to press ahead with Trump family-linked coastal resort
video:video.video:description: Albanian Prime Minister Edi Rama is vowing to press ahead with a massive coastal development linked to Jared Kushner, U.S.

video:video.video:title: Fans erupt in celebrations as Mexico beats Czech Republic 3-0 to complete perfect group stage
video:video.video:description: Thousands of Mexican fans celebrated in the rain after their team's victory over the Czech Republic 3-0 on Wednesday to reach the World Cup round of 32.

video:video.video:title: US ‘light artist’ opens his most daring skyspace yet
video:video.video:description: A monumental artwork based on Rome's Pantheon has been unveiled in Denmark. The dome by American “light artist” James Turrell is described as his most daring skyspace yet.

video:video.video:title: Early results show Swiss voters reject right-wing's bid to cap population at 10 million
video:video.video:description: Voters in Switzerland cast their final ballots on Sunday on an initiative championed by the top right-wing party to cap the rich Alpine country’s population at 10 million.

video:video.video:title: A behind the scenes look at the FBI's Joint Operations Center in NYC ahead of the World Cup
video:video.video:description: The World Cup, a 48-team, 104-match behemoth kicking off next week in 16 cities in the U.S., Mexico and Canada, presents an unprecedented security challenge.

video:video.video:title: Analysts assess how and if Andy Burnham will forge different path for UK than Keir Starmer
video:video.video:description: Andy Burnham, the former mayor of Greater Manchester who was sworn into Parliament hours after Keir Starmer announced his resignation on Monday, will be constrained by the platform the center-left Labour Party was elected on that decisively ended 14 years of Conservative rule in 2024.

video:video.video:title: Why Leo's first encyclical went viral and how it speaks to his papal approach
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV has issued a manifesto calling for robust regulation of artificial intelligence. His first encyclical, “Magnifica Humanitas,” has sparked widespread online reactions, with many young people praising his willingness to address AI's rapid rise.

video:video.video:title: UK and US diplomats stress Iran's influence in the Middle East extends to terror groups
video:video.video:description: The United Kingdom and the United States told members of the United Nations Security Council Wednesday that Iran's actions to halt traffic in the Strait of Hormuz must cease.

video:video.video:title: AP explains what does the signing of interim peace accord by Iran and US mean
video:video.video:description: An initial agreement to end the war between the United States and Iran calls for Tehran to dilute its stockpile of highly enriched uranium and would waive sanctions on the country, immediately allowing Iran to sell its oil freely, AP explains.

video:video.video:title: Spanish lawmakers give Pope 7-minute ovation after migration speech
video:video.video:description: Spanish lawmakers gave Pope Leo XIV a seven-minute standing ovation after he called for respect for migrants' rights and adherence to international law in the first papal address to Parliament.

video:video.video:title: Xi makes rare visit to Pyongyang as he and Kim seek stronger China-North Korea ties
video:video.video:description: China's President Xi Jinping and North Korea's leader Kim Jong Un underscored their commitment to deepen cooperation and rebuild their complicated traditional alliance.

video:video.video:title: 'Disclosure Day' star Emily Blunt believes in aliens
video:video.video:description: Actor Emily Blunt discusses the probability of extra-terrestrial life, as she joins director Steven Spielberg, Josh O'Connor, Colman Domingo and Eve Hewson at the New York premiere of "Disclosure Day."

video:video.video:title: Mayor Mamdani celebrates as New York Knicks win NBA championship
video:video.video:description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani celebrated the Knicks win at a bar in the West Village neighbourhood on Saturday night.

video:video.video:title: Displaced Nabatiyeh resident returns to find little of her home remains
video:video.video:description: Hanan Qubaisi climbs carefully over piles of concrete, twisted metal and broken furniture, searching through the rubble that was once her family home in the southern Lebanese city of Nabatiyeh.

video:video.video:title: Graham Platner wins Maine Democratic Senate nomination despite multiple controversies
video:video.video:description: Graham Platner has secured Maine's Democratic Senate nomination, setting up a high-stakes battle against longtime incumbent Republican Sen.

video:video.video:title: Peru police dress up as World Cup mascots during dramatic drugs raid
video:video.video:description: Peru's police have become well known for their targeted stings wearing eye-catching outfits. On Wednesday, they dressed up as 2026 World Cup mascots as they targeted a suspected drug trafficker.

video:video.video:title: Designing spacesuits for future Artemis moon landings
video:video.video:description: Artemis IV astronauts are getting a new spacesuit for their anticipated moon landing. Axiom Space and Prada have teamed up for the suit's design elements and seamstresses in Houston are laying down stitches for this and future space missions.

video:video.video:title: Fans celebrate as Egypt progresses to World Cup knockout rounds
video:video.video:description: Egypt soccer fans celebrated from Seattle to Cairo and in the camps of the Gaza Strip as the Pharaohs kept Iran to a 1-1 draw in their final Group B game in the World Cup advancing Egypt past the group stage at the World Cup for the first time.

video:video.video:title: Philadelphia mixes World Cup excitement with America 250th events
video:video.video:description: World Cup travelers to Philadelphia could find themselves stepping into two major celebrations at once.

video:video.video:title: Heat stress growing because of prolonged heatwaves in many nations, say researchers
video:video.video:description: Heatwaves around the world are getting hotter and lasting longer according to a new study. Scientists analyzed a global dataset of human heat stress using a measurement called the Universal Thermal Climate Index and found some subtropical countries are experiencing 50 more days per year of at least

video:video.video:title: Haiti World Cup squad member leaves Port-au-Prince to join teammates after being granted US visa
video:video.video:description: Supporters gathered around Woodensky Pierre as he arrived at the airport in Port-au-Prince on Tuesday, telling The AP that he had “a happy heart.”

video:video.video:title: Trump hails 'great' GOP Senate meeting after calling off bipartisan housing bill signing
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said he had a "really great meeting" with Senate Republicans Wednesday after a tense GOP luncheon and his abrupt cancellation of a bipartisan housing bill signing — the latest sign of friction with congressional Republicans.

video:video.video:title: NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani skips annual parade celebrating Israel but pledges big police presence
video:video.video:description: Crowds of people lined the streets in New York on Sunday to attend an annual parade honoring Israel.

video:video.video:title: What is an IPO? What to know as the potentially biggest IPO in history, SpaceX, hits the market
video:video.video:description: Anyone following SpaceX's plans to sell shares to the public is likely to hear terms thrown around that describe steps and components of an initial public offering.

video:video.video:title: A prayer book, a secret bunker, tunnel and a Star of David tell a story of Jewish resistance in a Polish town
video:video.video:description: A worn prayer book, a secret bunker, and an underground tunnel used as a hideout by Jews resisting the Nazis, are among rare findings in a house in Bedzin, in southern Poland.

video:video.video:title: Taylor Swift, Kenny Loggins, Alanis Morissette arrive at Songwriters Hall of Fame ceremony
video:video.video:description: Taylor Swift, Kiss' Gene Simmons and Paul Stanley, Christopher “Tricky” Stewart, Alanis Morissette and Kenny Loggins make up the 2026 Songwriters Hall of Fame inductees.

video:video.video:title: Desperate Venezuelans search quake rubble as anger grows over response
video:video.video:description: Desperation set in on Saturday among Venezuelans trying to locate their loved ones alive among piles of rubble, where public housing buildings and ocean view towers stood until twin powerful earthquakes brought them down midweek.

video:video.video:title: Rwanda's royal cows are treated to poetry and cultural reverence
video:video.video:description: Rwanda’s renowned Inyambo cows graze at the King’s Palace Museum, symbolizing the nation’s rich cultural heritage.

video:video.video:title: Two arrested in Italy after farm workers killed in apparent torching of car
video:video.video:description: Italian prosecutors were continuing an investigation on Wednesday following the arrests of two people accused in the deaths of four migrant farmworkers who were burned alive in a car.

video:video.video:title: Nvidia's Jensen Huang discusses AI policy, manufacturing and Trump relationship
video:video.video:description: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang stressed in an Associated Press interview Tuesday that society needs to change with the advent of AI, arguing that a fuller embrace of the technology would improve people's lives.

video:video.video:title: Toronto ready to welcome 300,000 World Cup visitors to Canada’s largest city
video:video.video:description: Toronto will host six World Cup games, starting Friday. Canada’s largest city is welcoming the world and emphasizing its diversity. (AP Video by Mike Householder)

video:video.video:title: Malaysia enforces social media ban for children under 16
video:video.video:description: Malaysia on Monday began enforcing rules barring millions of children younger than 16 from having social media accounts, joining a global effort to tighten online safety protections for young users.

video:video.video:title: Taylor Swift arrives for the Songwriters Hall of Fame gala
video:video.video:description: Taylor Swift walked the red carpet at the Songwriters Hall of Fame gala in New York. At 36, she's the youngest woman to be inducted. (June 11)

video:video.video:title: Pope says he’s competing with another VIP in Madrid this weekend: Puerto Rican sensation Bad Bunny
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV acknowledged on Saturday that he was competing with another VIP in Madrid this weekend.

video:video.video:title: Death Cab for Cutie's new album is about grief — and it's for the fans
video:video.video:description: "The fans are always right." Ben Gibbard focused on fan response when crafting songs for Death Cab for Cutie's 11th studio album, “I Built You a Tower.”

video:video.video:title: Labour’s Andy Burnham wins a UK special election, setting up a likely push to oust Prime Minister Keir Starmer
video:video.video:description: Labour’s Andy Burnham, the current mayor of Greater Manchester, has won a special election for a seat in Parliament that puts him in a position to challenge embattled Prime Minister Keir Starmer.

video:video.video:title: No maps, no insurance: Michigan floods expose lack of information, preparation in many rural areas
video:video.video:description: Many homeowners hit by devastating Michigan floods had no insurance and no idea they were at risk — underscoring vulnerabilities throughout rural America as climate change causes more extreme weather.

video:video.video:title: What to know as the House rebukes Trump with vote to limit war powers
video:video.video:description: The House for the first time Wednesday approved a war powers resolution that would halt the U.S. military action against Iran, defying President Donald Trump as a handful of Republicans joined with Democrats to end the three-month-long conflict.

video:video.video:title: Thousands of Vespa riders join Rome parade as iconic scooter turns 80
video:video.video:description: Over 10,000 Vespas moved around the Colosseum and past the Roman Forum on Saturday, marking the 80th anniversary of the iconic scooter as enthusiasts gathered from continental Europe, northern England, San Francisco, Australia’s Gold Coast, the Philippines and more.

video:video.video:title: South Korea coach and team booed by angry fans at airport after World Cup exit
video:video.video:description: Former South Korea head coach Hong Myung-bo and nine of the 26 players in the South Korea squad landed at Incheon International Airport near Seoul under heavy security.

video:video.video:title: Britain detains sanctioned oil tanker believed to be linked to Russia’s shadow fleet
video:video.video:description: Armed British forces boarded and detained a sanctioned tanker Sunday that is suspected of being part of the Russian “shadow fleet,” shipping oil in violation of international sanctions over Moscow’s war on Ukraine, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Sunday.

video:video.video:title: São Paulo Pride Parade draws crowds with focus on political engagement
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of thousands of people filled São Paulo's Paulista Avenue on Sunday for the 30th edition of the city's LGBTQ+ Pride Parade.

video:video.video:title: Indonesia's first giant panda cub Rio makes public debut at zoo in Cisarua
video:video.video:description: Panda enthusiasts flocked to Cisarua Zoo in Indonesia on Saturday to catch a glimpse of giant panda cub, Rio, the first of its kind in the country.

video:video.video:title: Trump is attending NBA Finals Game 3 between Knicks and Spurs
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump, a longtime New York Knicks fan, is attending the first NBA Finals game in New York since 1999.
